Title: GTM Data Analyst Intern
Description:
We are looking for a GTM Data Analyst Intern to join our Go-To-Market (GTM) Analytics team. In this role, you will work at the intersection of business and data, supporting critical initiatives that drive decision-making across Sales, Marketing, Finance and Revenue Operations.

This internship is designed as a hands-on, high-ownership experience centered around building new and/or migrating existing business-critical dashboards from Looker to Tableau, analyzing data models, performing data validation and producing documentation. You will deeply understand the underlying data, validate business logic, and improve the overall analytics foundation.

The ideal candidate is analytically curious, detail-oriented, and eager to learn how modern data teams operate. You should be comfortable working with data, asking questions, and translating business needs into structured analysis.

Getting started
We want you to feel like part of the team early on! Our onboarding process will integrate you into the company with informative sessions on our product, policies, processes, and team structure and goals.
We’re excited for you to learn, grow, and contribute right away! We trust that you’ll bring experience and knowledge that will uplift and uplevel the team, but we don’t expect you to know everything on Day 1.
What You'll Do.
Dashboard Migration: Own the migration of dashboards from Looker to Tableau. Recreate charts, metrics, and filters while ensuring consistency with existing outputs. Develop a deep understanding of underlying queries and business logic to ensure accuracy.
End-to-End Dashboard Development: Design and build a new Tableau dashboard from scratch using the data models you helped develop. Apply best practices in data visualization to deliver clear, actionable insights.
Data Modeling & Transformation: Work with dbt to transform raw data into clean, analysis-ready models. Standardize metric definitions, optimize data structures for performance, and ensure models are scalable for business use.
Data Validation & QA: Validate dashboard outputs against business expectations. Reconcile discrepancies between Looker and Tableau, test edge cases (date ranges, filters, segments), and identify logic or aggregation mismatches.
Dashboard Documentation: Document key dashboards, including purpose, stakeholders, KPIs, and filters. Define metric logic clearly and capture dependencies across data sources and pipelines to improve transparency and maintainability.
Data Pipeline Review & Gap Analysis: Review upstream data pipelines to understand how data flows into dashboards. Identify gaps such as missing data, delays, or schema inconsistencies, and recommend improvements to enhance data reliability.
Final Presentation & Insights: Synthesize your work into a structured presentation. Share key learnings, data quality findings, improvements made, and insights uncovered. Provide recommendations for future GTM analytics investments.
About you
Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, or a related field.
You are available for a 8-10 week full-time internship during the Summer 2026 cycle.
Strong foundational knowledge of SQL
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Looker is required
Basic understanding of data modeling or transformation concepts and exposure to dbt and Github
Familiarity with cloud data warehouses such as Snowflake or BigQuery
Strong attention to detail with the ability to validate data and identify inconsistencies.
Comfortable working with structured and unstructured data to answer business questions.
Eagerness to learn how data systems work end-to-end. Willingness to take ownership of projects and work through ambiguity.
Ability to clearly document work and communicate findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
You are authorized to work in the United States without restrictions. Please note that at this time, Everlaw is not sponsoring employment visas for this role.
Benefits
This is a non-exempt role with an estimated hourly rate of $31 - $44/hr, depending on experience.
Hands-on experience with modern data tools including Tableau and dbt
Understanding of how data flows from raw sources to business dashboards
Experience in data validation, QA, and analytics best practices
Exposure to real-world GTM data and decision-making processes
Opportunity to present insights and recommendations to stakeholders
Perks
Oakland HQ: Work from our beautiful, vibrant office in downtown Oakland with a flexible, hybrid-friendly culture.
Fully Stocked Kitchen: Enjoy catered lunches, a variety of snacks, and premium coffee to keep you fueled.
Community & Culture: Engage with "Everlawyers" through various ERGs (Employee Resource Groups) and team-building events.
Wellness Focus: Access to onsite amenities and programs designed to support your physical and mental well-being.
Pursue Truth While Finding YoursAt Everlaw, we are deeply invested in pursuing the truth, for our clients and for our employees. We know that when you’re empowered to pursue your passions, it is reflected in the work. That’s why we’re committed to the professional growth of all our team members, offering an annual learning and development stipend and regular career check-ins with managers. If you’re looking for a place that values passion, integrity, and a desire to learn, we’d love to hear from you!  ​​About EverlawWe help law firms, government agencies, and corporations sift through millions of documents of evidence in big lawsuits and investigations to find the proverbial smoking gun (or needle in the haystack -- pick your metaphor). It's a multi-billion dollar space typically dominated by service-oriented vendors, and we're coming at it with cutting-edge technology and elegant design. It's working, and we've been growing very rapidly: we host hundreds of terabytes of data and work with all 50 state Attorneys General and hundreds of law firms on some of the most high-profile cases litigated today.  Everlaw is an equal opportunity employer. We pride ourselves on having a diverse workforce and we do not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law. We respect the gender, gender identity and gender expression of our applicants and employees, and we honor requests for pronouns. It is our policy to comply with all applicable national, state and local laws pertaining to nondiscrimination and equal opportunity, including the California Equal Pay Act.  Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. We collect and process the personal information you provided along with your job application in accordance with our Applicants Privacy Notice and Notice at Collection. When preparing to engage with Everlaw as a candidate, you may use AI tools for research, polishing application materials, and interview prep. However, any assessments (unless explicitly stated), remote interviews or live interviews must be completed independently without AI support. By submitting your application, you agree to adhere to these rules. Here's the link to our full policy, and please reach out with any questions! We use Covey as part of our hiring and/or promotional processes. As part of the evaluation process, we provide Covey with job requirements and candidate-submitted applications. Certain features of the platform may qualify it as an Automated Employment Decision Tool (AEDT) under applicable regulations. For positions in New York City, our use of Covey complies with NYC Local Law 144. We began using Covey Scout for Inbound on the 9th of June, 2025.

Title: Sr. Analyst, Product Analytics
Description:
Why Sony Interactive Entertainment?

Sony Interactive Entertainment isn’t just the Best Place to Play — it’s also the Best Place to Work. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the company behind the PlayStation brand. As a subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation, we’re part of a proud legacy of innovation and excellence. SIE is a dynamic technology company, delivering cutting-edge hardware and network services to more than 100 million people and an entertainment leader, home to some of the most beloved and recognizable intellectual properties (IP) in the world. Our role at SIE is to create and nurture the experiences under the PlayStation brand, a name synonymous with entertainment excellence and creativity.

Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC seeks a Sr. Analyst, Product Analytics in San Mateo, CA to support the planning and rollout of large-scale testing strategies, including segmentation, global holdouts, and longitudinal studies. Requires a Master’s degree in Business Analytics, or related field or equivalent, and three (3) years of experience developing and deploying machine learning modeling for user segmentation, behavior prediction, and fraud detection using Random Forest, Gradient Boosted Trees (XGBoost), Logistic Regression, K-Means, DBSCAN, Latent Class Analysis, and association rule mining; designing and analyzing experiments across A/B testing, multivariate testing, frequentist frameworks, and Bayesian experimentation; designing multi-dimensional test structures and interpreting results across user segments; mitigating validity threats including SRM, contamination, and multi-exposure through stratified sampling and CUPED adjustment; performing causal impact measurement and competitor analysis using quasi-experimental methods including Synthetic Control Models, and time-series techniques including Difference-in-Differences, Interrupted Time Series, Bayesian Structural Time Series models, Seasonal-Trend Decomposition, and Rolling Regression; building and maintaining dashboards using Tableau/DOMO to integrate ETL processes; troubleshooting technical configurations, managing data pipelines, validating metrics, and supporting experimentation scalability; utilizing digital user behavior and monetization analytics, including online customer journeys, website conversions, payment-related user interactions, retention analysis, churn modeling, and marketing return on investment (ROI); applying data mining and behavioral segmentation techniques to uncover patterns that drive product, marketing, and revenue optimization decisions; and utilizing Python, R, SQL, Adobe Analytics, Customer Journey Analytics, and cloud-based data platforms including Databricks, Data Ocean, Redshift, AWS S3, MongoDB, data catalog and Postgres to manage and analyze large-scale datasets. Telecommuting and/or working from home may be permissible pursuant to company policies. Sony is an EOE.

Salary range: $175,013.00 – $248,900.00/year

Please note, Sony Interactive Entertainment conducts background checks at the offer stage for all new employees (which may include criminal background checks for some roles) and will need to process personal information to support these checks.

Please refer to our Candidate Privacy Notice for more information about what personal information we collect, how we use it, who we share it with, and your data protection rights.

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Sony is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All persons will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender (including gender identity, gender expression and gender reassignment), race (including colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin), religion or belief, marital or civil partnership status, disability, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity or parental status, trade union membership or membership in any other legally protected category.

We strive to create an inclusive environment, empower employees and embrace diversity. We encourage everyone to respond.

Sony Interactive Entertainment is a Fair Chance employer and qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records will be considered for employment.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Title: Senior Business Analyst - Regulatory Surveillance & AI-Enabled Analytics
Location: Rockville, MD or McLean, VA
Target Start Date: ASAP
Type: contract
Pay Rate: DOE

The Senior Business Analyst supports the analysis, enhancement, testing, and operational support of automated surveillance systems designed to detect potential regulatory violations across multiple asset classes and compliance domains. This role bridges business, regulatory, and technical teams by translating surveillance requirements into scalable analytical solutions and validating that implemented logic produces accurate, defensible results.

The ideal candidate combines strong data analysis and SQL expertise with the ability to interpret complex processing logic, investigate data anomalies, and support surveillance modernization initiatives. This role also requires hands-on experience leveraging Generative AI tools and AI-assisted workflows to improve analysis, documentation, testing, and operational efficiency.

Core ResponsibilitiesSurveillance Analysis & Requirements Validation
Analyze and reverse-engineer surveillance pattern logic, workflows, and data pipelines
Develop and maintain detailed business and technical documentation for surveillance patterns and regulatory workflows
Perform gap analysis between business requirements and implemented logic to identify inconsistencies, risks, or missing coverage
Support enhancements and modernization efforts across surveillance and compliance platforms
UAT, Testing & Release Support
Validate production versus UAT outputs to ensure surveillance accuracy and consistency
Design and execute complex test scenarios, including edge cases and exception handling
Build and maintain local testing environments for independent validation of surveillance logic and data processing workflows
Support release management activities including QC validation, documentation packaging, and deployment readiness
Data Analysis & Investigation Support
Write complex SQL queries to analyze large-scale datasets and investigate surveillance exceptions or anomalies
Conduct firm-level trend analysis, operational reporting, and statistical reviews
Assess downstream impacts of data incidents and dependency changes across surveillance systems
Maintain and support reference data used in analytical and surveillance workflows
Automation & Tool Development
Develop scripts, utilities, and automation tools to improve operational workflows, reporting, testing, and documentation processes
Build comparison and visualization tools to support release validation and data reconciliation activities
Support workflow optimization initiatives through automation and AI-assisted development practices
Generative AI & AI-Assisted Workflows
Utilize AI coding assistants and Generative AI tools to accelerate code analysis, documentation generation, testing, and operational tasks
Design structured AI workflows, prompt frameworks, and reusable project contexts for analytical and documentation activities
Validate AI-generated outputs for accuracy, regulatory alignment, and operational reliability
Champion adoption of AI-enabled productivity tools and best practices across the team
Project & Stakeholder Support
Support project tracking, enhancement requests, defect management, and release coordination activities
Assist in drafting and maintaining Business Requirements Documents (BRDs) and functional documentation
Collaborate with engineering, compliance, regulatory, and operational stakeholders throughout the SDLC lifecycle
Required Qualifications
Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field
5+ years of experience in regulatory technology, surveillance systems, financial services, or large-scale data analysis environments
Strong SQL expertise with experience analyzing large or distributed datasets
Experience with scripting or programming languages such as Python or similar technologies
Ability to interpret complex business logic, data processing pipelines, and technical workflows
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience investigating data discrepancies and validating system outputs
Excellent communication and documentation skills with the ability to translate technical concepts into business-friendly documentation
Advanced proficiency with spreadsheet and reporting tools
Preferred Qualifications
Knowledge of SEC, Client, or financial regulatory surveillance processes

Title: Senior Business Analyst - Regulatory Surveillance & AI-Enabled Analytics
Location: Rockville, MD or McLean, VA
Target Start Date: ASAP
Type: contract
Pay Rate: DOE

The Senior Business Analyst supports the analysis, enhancement, testing, and operational support of automated surveillance systems designed to detect potential regulatory violations across multiple asset classes and compliance domains. This role bridges business, regulatory, and technical teams by translating surveillance requirements into scalable analytical solutions and validating that implemented logic produces accurate, defensible results.

The ideal candidate combines strong data analysis and SQL expertise with the ability to interpret complex processing logic, investigate data anomalies, and support surveillance modernization initiatives. This role also requires hands-on experience leveraging Generative AI tools and AI-assisted workflows to improve analysis, documentation, testing, and operational efficiency.

Core ResponsibilitiesSurveillance Analysis & Requirements Validation
  • Analyze and reverse-engineer surveillance pattern logic, workflows, and data pipelines
  • Develop and maintain detailed business and technical documentation for surveillance patterns and regulatory workflows
  • Perform gap analysis between business requirements and implemented logic to identify inconsistencies, risks, or missing coverage
  • Support enhancements and modernization efforts across surveillance and compliance platforms
UAT, Testing & Release Support
  • Validate production versus UAT outputs to ensure surveillance accuracy and consistency
  • Design and execute complex test scenarios, including edge cases and exception handling
  • Build and maintain local testing environments for independent validation of surveillance logic and data processing workflows
  • Support release management activities including QC validation, documentation packaging, and deployment readiness
Data Analysis & Investigation Support
  • Write complex SQL queries to analyze large-scale datasets and investigate surveillance exceptions or anomalies
  • Conduct firm-level trend analysis, operational reporting, and statistical reviews
  • Assess downstream impacts of data incidents and dependency changes across surveillance systems
  • Maintain and support reference data used in analytical and surveillance workflows
Automation & Tool Development
  • Develop scripts, utilities, and automation tools to improve operational workflows, reporting, testing, and documentation processes
  • Build comparison and visualization tools to support release validation and data reconciliation activities
  • Support workflow optimization initiatives through automation and AI-assisted development practices
Generative AI & AI-Assisted Workflows
  • Utilize AI coding assistants and Generative AI tools to accelerate code analysis, documentation generation, testing, and operational tasks
  • Design structured AI workflows, prompt frameworks, and reusable project contexts for analytical and documentation activities
  • Validate AI-generated outputs for accuracy, regulatory alignment, and operational reliability
  • Champion adoption of AI-enabled productivity tools and best practices across the team
Project & Stakeholder Support
  • Support project tracking, enhancement requests, defect management, and release coordination activities
  • Assist in drafting and maintaining Business Requirements Documents (BRDs) and functional documentation
  • Collaborate with engineering, compliance, regulatory, and operational stakeholders throughout the SDLC lifecycle
Required Qualifications
  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field
  • 5+ years of experience in regulatory technology, surveillance systems, financial services, or large-scale data analysis environments
  • Strong SQL expertise with experience analyzing large or distributed datasets
  • Experience with scripting or programming languages such as Python or similar technologies
  • Ability to interpret complex business logic, data processing pipelines, and technical workflows
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience investigating data discrepancies and validating system outputs
  • Excellent communication and documentation skills with the ability to translate technical concepts into business-friendly documentation
  • Advanced proficiency with spreadsheet and reporting tools
Preferred Qualifications
  • Knowledge of SEC, Client, or financial regulatory surveillance processes
  • Experience with distributed data platforms and big data ecosystems
  • Familiarity with source control, release management, and SDLC workflows
  • Experience with project tracking and documentation management tools
  • Understanding of surveillance architectures including exception generation, aggregation, scoring, and reconciliation processes
  • Experience leveraging Generative AI tools, LLMs, or AI-assisted coding platforms within analytical or operational workflows
  • Ability to design effective AI prompts and structured workflows in regulated environments

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates a network of daily local news podcasts, newsletters, and websites across multiple U.S. cities. It addresses the challenge of keeping communities informed by delivering timely local news and information through accessible audio and digital formats. The network spans cities including Chicago, DC, Denver, Las Vegas, Madison, Nashville, Philadelphia, Pittsburgh, Portland, Salt Lake, Seattle, and Twin Cities, offering a comprehensive local news resource.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Data Analys role is critical for integrating audience and revenue data to support strategic decision-making. This position is responsible for consolidating various data sources into actionable insights, forecasting growth, and helping different departments understand performance metrics. The role enables the organization to optimize operations, editorial content, and revenue strategies by providing clear and timely data analysis.




3. Responsibilities:

• Manage audience and revenue data from ingestion through reporting and storytelling.

• Define and maintain key performance metrics in collaboration with leadership.

• Collaborate with operations, membership, revenue, finance, and editorial teams to develop a cohesive data ecosystem.

• Build and maintain unified dashboards integrating data from podcast platforms, newsletters, web analytics, CRM, and subscription systems.

• Create customized dashboards for different stakeholders including executives, editorial, and revenue teams.

• Forecast audience growth by market and business line; validate forecasts against actual performance.

• Develop and maintain first-party data strategies across platforms.

• Provide regular insights and analysis to staff via reports, presentations, and informal coaching.

• Utilize AI tools to enhance dashboard development, reporting automation, and data pattern identification.

• Support targeted analyses for decisions on pricing, new market launches, format experiments, and advertising performance.




4. Requirements:

• Minimum 4 years’ experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or audience analytics, preferably in media.

• Understanding of newsroom dynamics, audience growth, and revenue models including advertising and subscriptions.

• Proficiency in SQL and at least one BI or visualization tool (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI).

• Experience with Python or R is advantageous.

• Skilled in applying AI tools to accelerate and enhance data workflows.

• Proven ability to build effective, user-friendly dashboards.

• Strong communication skills for presenting data insights to non-technical audiences.

• Self-directed and capable of establishing data structures independently.

• Commitment to supporting local journalism and community engagement.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Salary range is $80,000 to $100,000, depending on experience.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• This position is full-time and remote with excellent benefits.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Location : Cook, IL-Onsite (Day 1 Onsite)

Duration: 6-12 months

Job Description:

Work closely with business stakeholders to gather, validate, and clarify data and reporting requirements.
Translate business needs into detailed functional and technical requirement documents.
Develop comprehensive Source-to-Target Mapping (STTM/STM) documents capturing field-level data logic.

Perform complete data lineage analysis across Snowflake, APIs, upstream sources, and reporting layers.

Collaborate with data engineers, ETL/ELT developers, and QA to ensure accurate interpretation of the requirements.
Support UAT and resolve data-related gaps through iterative testing and validation.
Mentor and guide more junior team members.




Skills

Proven experience with programming languages such as SQL and Python for data validation, profiling, reconciliation, and analytics.
Strong hands-on Snowflake experience is required, including: Ability to construct & optimize complex SQL queries o Variant/JSON parsing o Schema and stage understanding o Performance analysis & troubleshooting
Working experience in Medallion Architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold data layers) including mapping data flows across layers.
Proven experience with API-based data ingestion (REST APIs, JSON/XML nice to have

Title: Data Quality Analyst - Data Governance
Description:
A Data Governance Data Quality Assurance (QA) professional ensures the accuracy, consistency, and security of enterprise data by implementing, monitoring, and enforcing data policies, standards, and validation rules. Title: Junior Data Engineer
Description:
About the Role

We are looking for an enthusiastic Junior Data Engineer to join our growing data team. In this role, you will help build, maintain, and optimize our data pipelines and architecture. This is an excellent opportunity for an early-career engineer to work with modern data tools, learn from senior mentorship, and help turn raw data into actionable business insights.




What You’ll Do

Pipeline Support: Assist in building and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines to move and transform data.
Data Quality: Monitor data pipelines, identify inconsistencies, and write validation scripts to ensure data accuracy.
Database Maintenance: Support the management of data warehouses and data lakes for downstream analytics.
Collaboration: Partner with data analysts and data scientists to understand their data requirements and deliver clean datasets.
Documentation: Create and update documentation for data schemas, pipeline workflows, and system architectures.




What You’ll Bring

Core Skills: Solid foundational knowledge of SQL and programming languages like Python or Scala.
Data Concepts: Understanding of relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL) and basic data warehousing concepts.
Tools & Platforms: Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) or data tools (dbt, Airflow, Spark) is a strong asset.
Mindset: A strong desire to learn, great attention to detail, and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates in the AI lending marketplace, collaborating with banks and credit unions to expand access to affordable credit through advanced technology. It addresses challenges in credit accessibility by leveraging extensive predictive models and numerous data signals to enable fairer lending decisions. The company supports a digital-first work environment with a presence across multiple US cities and Canada, promoting flexible work arrangements alongside intentional in-person collaboration.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Staff Data Analyst serves as a strategic analytics partner to Operations and Product leaders in the servicing analytics team. The role focuses on shaping servicing strategy by delivering data-driven insights and developing scalable reporting solutions. The position requires independent problem-solving across complex, evolving business issues and influences decision-making through clear analytical communication.




3. Responsibilities:

• Partner with Operations, Product Management, and leadership to define success metrics and evaluate performance

• Build and maintain scalable dashboards and reporting frameworks for self-service analytics

• Conduct root cause analyses on borrower and operational metrics and recommend actions

• Lead analytical support for product experiments and operational initiatives

• Identify opportunities to improve operational efficiency and borrower outcomes

• Present complex analytical findings to senior stakeholders




4. Requirements:

• Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or related field plus 6 years of experience

• 8+ years in data or business analytics supporting business operations or product organizations

• Advanced SQL skills for complex queries and dataset transformations

• Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, or similar for dashboards and reporting

• Proven ability in root cause analysis, defining performance metrics, and delivering recommendations

• Preferred: knowledge of consumer lending or financial services domains

• Preferred: experience with product experiments or A/B testing

• Preferred: strong communication skills for senior leadership

• Preferred: use of AI-enabled tools or automation for reporting and analysis




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Medical insurance

• Dental insurance

• Vision insurance

• 401(k)

Title: Data Entry Specialist, Remote
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization is a national leader in service of process with a team of 1000 employees and multiple offices across the United States, including Los Angeles, Oklahoma City, Phoenix, Brooklyn, Chicago, Washington DC, and headquarters in Seattle. With over 30 years of experience in the industry, the organization continuously advances its technology and business processes to maintain a competitive edge. The company focuses on expanding its technological leadership, acquiring and integrating less efficient competitors, and entering new market segments through a coordinated inbound marketing and sales strategy.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Data Entry Specialist role involves reviewing and filing legal documents using online platforms and tools developed internally. The position supports the e-Fulfillment and e-Filing teams by collaborating on projects, resolving issues, and achieving shared objectives. This remote role requires the candidate to be located in Virginia.




3. Responsibilities:

• Review and file legal documents through internal systems and email

• Participate in ongoing training to increase industry and process knowledge

• Investigate discrepancies as they occur

• Complete additional assigned projects




4. Requirements:

• No prior experience required; data entry experience is advantageous

• Strong preference for candidates with technical experience

• Proficiency in English (reading, writing, speaking)

• High school diploma or GED

• Ability to perform repetitive tasks accurately

• High attention to detail

• Team player mentality

• Basic proficiency with Microsoft Office

• Typing speed between 50 to 60 words per minute




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Pay range: $15.00 per hour




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Health, Dental, and Vision insurance

• 401(k) plan with company matching

• Paid time off

• Seven paid company holidays

• Four floating holidays per year

• Life Insurance and AD&D Insurance

• Long Term Disability

• Health Care Reimbursement Flexible Spending Account

• Dependent Care Flexible Spending Account

• Employee Assistance Program

• Pet Insurance

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. Title: Market Insights Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Business Analyst (Analyst, Competitive Strategy & Insights)

Location: Remote

Duration: 6 Months with possible extension

Max Pay rate: $49/hr




Key Responsibilities

Lead international market sizing analyses across priority regions using multiple data sources, including internal data, third-party research, web data, and industry benchmarks.
Build robust bottom-up and top-down models in Excel to estimate Total Addressable Market (TAM), category penetration, growth opportunities, and market trends.
Analyze financial statements, annual reports, and industry publications to identify key trends and develop business models.
Evaluate competitor performance and positioning across regions, including traffic trends, GMV proxies, pricing strategies, supply dynamics, and assortment.
Design and maintain standardized Excel frameworks to track market size, growth rates, and competitor performance metrics over time.
Create clean, scalable datasets by aggregating, validating, and normalizing data from multiple internal and external sources.
Conduct quantitative benchmarking across competitors, markets, and categories to assess relative market position and identify opportunities.
Perform in-depth desktop research and synthesize findings into quantitative models and qualitative insights that inform business strategy and performance.
Manage external vendor relationships to ensure high-quality data delivery, while proactively identifying gaps and driving continuous improvements.
Partner with leadership and cross-functional teams to provide data-driven recommendations for strategic planning and decision-making.
Manage multiple concurrent projects with clear plans, stakeholder alignment, and timely delivery of key milestones and deliverables.

What You Need to Succeed

Bachelor’s degree (BA/BS) with 5+ years of experience in strategy, analytics, consulting, or e-commerce insights.
Strong business acumen with a solid understanding of e-commerce or marketplace models, including buyer/seller dynamics, pricing, assortment, and conversion drivers.
Advanced Excel skills with strong modeling and analytical capabilities.
Excellent structured problem-solving and quantitative reasoning skills.
Proven ability to convert complex data into clear insights and compelling executive-level presentations.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence mid-level and senior leaders.
Experience working cross-functionally in fast-paced, dynamic, and ambiguous environments.
Strong project management and organizational skills, with the ability to prioritize and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.
High ownership mindset, intellectual curiosity, and a continuous improvement approach.
Collaborative, positive, and easy-to-work-with attitude.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in e-commerce or marketplace platforms.
Proficiency in SQL for data extraction, querying, and analysis.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Looker.
Experience working with large, complex datasets.
MBA or equivalent advanced degree.

Title: Data Research Analyst
Description:
SYSTEMTEC is seeking a Data Reporting / Research Analyst for a remote opportunity for candidates in the EST. The Candidate will support survey administration, reporting operations, and institutional data initiatives for a higher education organization. This role is responsible for coordinating survey schedules, managing evaluation processes, monitoring participation metrics, and ensuring the accuracy and integrity of reporting data.




Required Qualifications for the Data Reporting / Research Analyst:

Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S. We are unable to provide sponsorship or work with Third-Party agencies.
Bachelor’s degree from an accredited institution is required
2+ years of experience administering surveys and developing data reports
Experience working with survey management platforms such as Qualtrics, SmartEvals, SogoSurvey, Alchemer, or similar tools
2+ years of experience using Power BI, Tableau, or similar data visualization tools
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including Pivot Tables and reporting functions
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills
Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
Experience monitoring data quality and ensuring reporting accuracy
Must currently reside within the Central or Eastern Time Zones




Preferred Skills/Experience of the Data Reporting / Research Analyst:

Experience using SQL or similar data extraction and querying tools
Prior experience supporting reporting or survey initiatives within higher education environments
Familiarity with course evaluation or institutional research processes




Responsibilities of the Data Reporting/Research Analyst:

Coordinate and maintain survey distribution schedules and reporting timelines
Manage course evaluation processes from setup through completion and reporting
Monitor survey participation rates and assist with improving response outcomes
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across reporting platforms
Support the creation and maintenance of institutional reports and online reporting resources
Assist with additional survey, reporting, and analytics projects as needed




Work Location: Remote — Candidates must currently reside in Eastern Time Zones.




Compensation / Benefits:

Full-Time Employment with SYSTEMTEC means a competitive salary + paid OT, PTO, holidays, health, dental, disability, and life coverage, 401K, tuition reimbursement and more




***Please note: SYSTEMTEC is not set up to employ workers in the states of California, New York, and New Jersey.***

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job#: 3034483

Job Description:

Apex Systems is hiring a Data Analyst for a large healthcare client.

Location: Fully remote anywhere in the Unted States.

Employment Type: 6-month contract with possibility to extend or convert.

Role Overview

We are seeking a highly analytical Data Analyst to support a large enterprise healthcare organization with analytics initiatives spanning Commercial, Medicare, and Medicaid populations. This role will focus on generating actionable datasets, supporting ad hoc business requests, and enabling predictive modeling initiatives such as member-level risk scoring. This is a hands-on data role requiring advanced SQL expertise, strong analytical curiosity, and the ability to explore and validate complex healthcare datasets.

Key Responsibilities


Write highly complex SQL queries across Databricks and legacy Oracle systems, utilizing advanced constructs like multi-table JOINs, nested subqueries, CTEs, and window functions.
Build member-level analytic datasets by synthesizing claims, eligibility, utilization, and risk data.
Troubleshoot and refine queries for performance and accuracy in large-scale environments.
Construct clean, structured datasets to support predictive modeling use cases, including feature engineering from raw data.
Validate dataset integrity prior to model training through null analysis, duplication checks, and distribution validation.
Partner with Data Scientists to ensure features align with modeling objectives.
Perform deep exploratory analysis to uncover trends, patterns, anomalies, and data quality issues in healthcare datasets.
Translate ambiguous stakeholder requests into structured queries and datasets.
Develop recurring extracts and analytic views for operational and executive reporting.
Collaborate with BI Developers to ensure reporting accuracy and consistency.


Required Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Information Systems, or a related field.

Technical Skills: Strong SQL skills are required, with the ability to write large, complex queries quickly and accurately. Experience with Python for analysis and visualization (e.g., pandas, matplotlib) is also necessary.

Experience: Experience working with relational databases and understanding table relationships is required. Candidates must demonstrate strong analytical thinking, curiosity, and the ability to interpret healthcare datasets and explain findings clearly.

Preferred Qualifications


Master’s Degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Public Health, or a related field.
PySpark experience.
Basic data modeling knowledge.
Experience with Databricks.
Exposure to healthcare claims or population health data.
Familiarity with basic data pipeline scheduling and transformations.


Work Environment

This is a fully remote position open to candidates located anywhere in the United States. The expected work schedule is Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Pacific Standard Time.

We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.

Apex uses a virtual recruiter as part of the application process. Click here for more details.

If you have visited our website in search of information on employment opportunities or to apply for a position, and you require an accommodation in using our website for a search or application, please contact our Benefits Department at [email protected] or 804-523-8228.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
The Business Data Analyst plays a critical role in supporting commercial excellence by gathering, analyzing, and interpreting internal and external data to generate actionable insights that inform business decisions. This role partners with sales, marketing, contracting, and cross-functional teams; both local and global; to identify key performance trends, support strategic initiatives, and deliver high-quality analytical deliverables. The incumbent will design and maintain reporting templates, develop dashboards, conduct market and customer analyses, and help drive process improvements. The Business Analyst serves as a trusted analytics resource, communicating findings to stakeholders and contributing to the commercialization and operational excellence of the organization.




This is a hybrid position, and the selected candidate will be required to work onsite in the Lake Zurich, Illinois office 3 days a week.




Responsibilities




Data Analysis & Reporting

Analyze commercial, operational, and market data to identify trends, patterns, opportunities, and risks that support strategic and tactical decision-making.
Develop and manage reporting templates, dashboards, and visualizations to communicate insights clearly to commercial teams.
Ensure the quality, accuracy, and business readiness of analytical outputs, including validation of data and results before dissemination.
Support commercial data governance best practices to maintain consistency and integrity of analytical outputs.




Commercial Insight Generation

Conduct advanced analytics to support performance measurement, brand planning, sales effectiveness, promotional evaluation, and segmentation.
Prepare and present actionable commercial insights, supporting leadership with recommendations that address market dynamics and competitive landscapes.
Analyze forecast accuracy, KPIs, call and sample data, and other performance metrics to identify drivers of commercial outcomes.




Cross-Functional Collaboration & Support

Partner with internal stakeholders including sales, marketing, contracting, finance, and IT to understand analytical needs and deliver tailored solutions.
Support business unit stakeholders by translating analytics into business cases, executive summaries, and strategic recommendations.
Collaborate with data, CRM, and BI teams to improve data workflows, reporting processes, and system integrations.




Process & Systems Improvement

Identify process improvement opportunities and propose solutions to enhance efficiency, data quality, and analytical capability.
Participate in user acceptance testing (UAT) for reporting tools and analytics systems, ensuring solutions function as intended.
Support enhancements to commercial data structures and reporting platforms to align with evolving business requirements.




Communication & Documentation

Communicate complex analytical findings verbally and in writing to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Document analytical processes, assumptions, and methodologies to ensure reproducibility and continuity.
Assist with preparing presentations for leadership reviews, business reviews, and strategic planning sessions.

Special Projects & Strategic Initiatives

Support strategic initiatives related to commercial operations, product launches, and cross-functional projects.
Conduct ad-hoc analyses and scenario modeling to evaluate business decisions, pricing impacts, market entry, and promotional strategies.




Additional Responsibilities

Attends local, national, and global meetings as needed.
Completes all training requirements, including all department-specific, compliance training, etc.
Participates in any and all reasonable work activities as assigned by management.
All employees are responsible for ensuring the compliance to company documents, programs and activities related to the Health, Safety, Environment, Energy, and Quality Management Systems, as per your roles and responsibilities.




Requirements




Bachelor's degree required (Advanced degree is a plus).
3-5 years of relevant experience (preferably in in healthcare or related industry).
Proficiency in analytics tools such as Qlik, Snowflake, Salesforce, SAP, Power BI, SQL, Python, or R.
Knowledge of medical device, hospital/pharmacy products and/or pharmaceutical industry is strongly preferred.
Highly self-driven, capable of working independently and collaboratively in dynamic conditions.
Able to manage and prioritize multiple complex projects with demonstrated time management skills.
Exceptional attention to detail.
Strong communication and presentation abilities, both written and verbal.
Strong team player that is solution-oriented and can build influential relationships with both internal department and external customers.
Experience presenting with strong interpersonal and communication skills (verbal and written).
Demonstrated ability to prioritize and execute tasks in a dynamic environment.
Ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external business contacts while conveying a positive, service-oriented attitude.
Ability to maintain complete confidentiality and discretion in business relationships and exercise sound business judgment.
Highest level of integrity and good judgment, with the ability to effectively deal with highly sensitive, confidential information.
Ability to work flexible hours and weekends to meet business/customer needs.




Additional Information




Salary Range: $65,000 - $85,000 per year

Final pay determinations will depend on various factors, including, but not limited to experience level, education, knowledge, skills, and abilities. Our benefits and programs are comprehensive and thoughtfully crafted to ensure our colleagues live healthy lives and have support when it matters most.




We offer an excellent salary and benefits package including medical, dental and vision coverage, as well as life insurance, disability, 401K with company contribution, and wellness program.




Please note that joining our team does not create a guaranteed or permanent employment arrangement. All employment is at‑will, meaning both the employee and Fresenius Kabi have the right to end the employment relationship at any time, in accordance with applicable federal and state laws.




Fresenius Kabi is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship, immigration status, disabilities, or protected veteran status.

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
At Acorns, our mission is to look after the financial best interests of the up-and-coming, beginning with the empowering, proud step of micro-investing. How do we accomplish our mission? Our values.

Lead With Heart - With compassion, integrity, and tenacity, inspire yourself and others to move past all previous thresholds of possibility

Make Bold Decisions - We are creating a new world. Be optimistic, adventurous, and courageous. Leap fearlessly into the future

Always Build Trust - Say what you mean, mean what you say, and do what you say you’re going to do. We are all owners. We are one team

Never Stop Growing - Change IS the constant. Stay hungry and curious. Be relentless in the pursuit of progress

Find a way - Never settle, no excuses, nothing is impossible. Just make it happen

Our values guide us, and our mission drives us. Come join us and help deliver financial wellness for the whole family, putting the tools of wealth-making into everyone’s hands.

Data Analyst I | Acorns

Acorns is seeking a Data Analyst I to serve as a key partner for our Core Experience team, specifically focused on our Investing and Banking product lines. In this role, you will leverage data to optimize the customer journey, working at the intersection of Product, Finance, and Operations to ensure our core features deliver maximum value to our customers. You will play a vital role in shaping the product roadmap by transforming raw data into actionable insights that drive retention and enhance the overall customer experience.

Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.

What You Will Do At Acorns


Collaborate with the Core Experience team to drive the ideation and rigorous analysis of product experiments.
Partner with Product, Finance, and Operations to build and maintain robust reports and dashboards specifically for our Core Experience product lines.
Write and optimize complex SQL queries to extract data from various sources for deep-dive analysis.
Utilize Databricks and DBT to transform raw data into clean, accessible datasets for the broader team.
Design and manage intuitive Tableau dashboards that track key performance indicators (KPIs) for Investing and Banking features.
Monitor customer behavior and product performance using Amplitude to identify opportunities for friction reduction and growth.
Work with managers and cross-functional teams to establish priorities, including new infrastructure and tool building.


What You Will Bring To Acorns


3-5 years of Analytics experience, preferably supporting product development or financial services.
Strong SQL skills for data extraction and experience with data visualization tools like Tableau.
A solid understanding of statistical concepts and hands-on experience of A/B testing methodologies and product experimentation.
Familiarity with modern data stack tools such as Databricks, DBT, and product analytics platforms like Amplitude.
The ability to translate complex numerical findings into clear, persuasive recommendations for non-technical stakeholders in Finance and Operations.
Hunger to deliver game-changing products.
Exceptional drive and precision in delivery.
A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.
Optimistic about the potential of societal change.


You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply.

What We Offer


Competitive salary and stock options
A comprehensive benefits package for you and your family
Flexible work location, hours, and paid time off
401(k) matching
Monthly Acorns account contribution & GoHenry account for your family
Mindfulness and Financial Wellness resources, Headspace and Addition Wealth
Acorns Career Development Program (Ongoing training sessions, development plans, development check-ins, Cornerstone’s online training platform)
Roots Leadership Program for Emerging Leaders
Community week onsite gatherings and various virtual events
Talented and motivated team members who care deeply about one another, our mission, and our customers.
The rare opportunity to create a new world. We inspire one another every day to do meaningful work that solves big societal challenges.


About Acorns

Acorns is a financial wellness app that helps everyday people and families save and invest money for the long term. Since 2014, Acorns has grown into a global company with multiple life-stage products serving the needs of kids, teens, adults, and parents. Named one of Time’s “World’s Best Brands of 2024,” Acorns has helped over 14 million people save & invest over $25 billion dollars, much of it from spare change and small amounts.

Compensation Information

The pay range for this position at commencement of employment is expected to be between $121,000 and $140,000/year; however, base pay offered may vary depending on multiple individualized factors, including market location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. The total compensation package for this position may also include other elements, including equity grants, and discretionary cash bonus awards in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits (including 401(k) eligibility and various paid time off benefits, such as vacation, sick time, and parental leave), dependent on the position offered. Details of participation in these benefit plans will be provided if an employee receives an offer of employment.

If hired, employee will be in an “at-will position” and the Company reserves the right to modify base salary (as well as any other discretionary payment or compensation program) at any time, including for reasons related to individual performance, Company or individual department/team performance, and market factors.

Work Authorization

Acorns participates in E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. For more information about E-Verify, please visit www.e-verify.gov

Title: Analyst - Data Science
Description:
Job Description

The AIM (Analytics, Investment & Marketing Enablement) team – a part of GCS Marketing– is the analytical engine that enables Global Commercial business portfolio of American Express. Accelerating growth momentum, increasing profitability, and powering up our value proposition are key objectives for this organization. It is the analytical engine that powers profitable business growth through consultative and innovative solutions across.

The team enables GCS Marketing business by providing actionable insights to drive business strategy and growth. Incumbent in this role would work very closely with Marketing organization in Marketing space to provide meaningful analytics and actionable insights that help in creating growth strategies. The Analyst would perform analysis to guide strategic decision, regular campaign measurements, ROI analysis and enable the business by delivering key insights.

Responsibilities

The roles and responsibilities of the position would include but not be limited to:


Develop analytical solutions for GCS Customer Marketing by gathering requirements from stakeholders, performing data analysis, tracking performance.
Accurate, timely and efficient delivery of monthly results reporting for strategic initiatives like Offer personalization, Orchestra
Support and enable the business partners with campaign measurements, ROI analysis and actionable data driven insights for acquisition strategy in GCS
Keen on exploration and analysis of data from various data sources, translate the same to excellent visual case studies.
Quick learner quick to understand the business and support pilot projects amidst ambiguity
Collaborate with functional leaders, business partners and senior leaders to assess business needs, evaluate project feasibility, ensure data integrity, and provide actionable recommendations in times of constant change
Build a solid relationship/partnership with Marketing Stakeholders, drive projects end to end with full accountability and growth mindset


Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:


Knowledge of Hive, Python, PySpark, SQL encompassing data manipulation and statistical modeling/data-mining techniques
Strong analytical aptitude and technical skills
Project Management skills
Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal communications skills with an ability to formulate and communicate results & methodologies in a clear and compelling way
A demonstrated ability to work in high-pressure situations with minimal errors
An optimistic and growth mind-set with a tremendous will to win
Should be able to function with minimum supervision with high sense of ownership
Strong technical and problem-solving skills
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to analyze large amounts of data and create innovative solutions
Ability to work effectively in a team environment
Ability to learn quickly and work independently with complex, unstructured initiatives
Ability to challenge the status quo and drive innovation


Preferred Qualification


Degree in a quantitative field preferred – e.g. B. Tech; BSc/MSc Statistics, Bachelors/master’s in economics or equivalent work experience
Experience in marketing analytics and campaign measurement preferred; relevant industry experience preferred
1+ years of relevant experience with strong knowledge of skills like Python, SQL & Advanced Excel required; R also good to have.
Good knowledge of statistical techniques like hypothesis testing, regression, XGBoost, KNN, t-test, chi-square test


Depending on factors such as business unit requirements, the nature of the position, cost and applicable laws, American Express may provide visa sponsorship for certain positions.

About Us

At American Express, our culture is built on a 175-year history of innovation, shared values and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. From delivering differentiated products to providing world-class customer service, we operate with a strong risk mindset, ensuring we continue to uphold our brand promise of trust, security, and service.

As part of Team Amex, you’ll experience our powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career. Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express.

About The Team

We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:


Competitive base salaries
Bonus incentives
6% Company Match on retirement savings plan
Free financial coaching and financial well-being support
Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits
Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need
20+ weeks paid parental leave for all parents, regardless of gender, offered for pregnancy, adoption or surrogacy
Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location)
Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program
Career development and training opportunities


For a full list of Team Amex benefits, visit out Colleague Benefits Site .

American Express is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, disability status, age, or any other status protected by law. American Express will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with arrest or conviction records, in accordance with the requirements of applicable state and local laws, including the California Fair Chance Act, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the City of Los Angeles’ Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance. For positions covered by federal and/or state banking regulations, American Express will comply with such regulations as it relates to the consideration of applicants with criminal convictions.

We back our colleagues with the support they need to thrive, professionally and personally. That's why we have Amex Flex, our enterprise working model that provides greater flexibility to colleagues while ensuring we preserve the important aspects of our unique in-person culture. Depending on role and business needs, colleagues will either work onsite, in a hybrid model (combination of in-office and virtual days) or fully virtually.

It is the analytical engine that powers profitable business growth through consultative and innovative solutions across.

The team enables GCS Marketing business by providing actionable insights to drive business strategy and growth. Incumbent in this role would work very closely with Marketing organization in Marketing space to provide meaningful analytics and actionable insights that help in creating growth strategies. The Analyst would perform analysis to guide strategic decision, regular campaign measurements, ROI analysis and enable the business by delivering key insights.

Responsibilities

The roles and responsibilities of the position would include but not be limited to:

  • Develop analytical solutions for GCS Customer Marketing by gathering requirements from stakeholders, performing data analysis, tracking performance.
  • Accurate, timely and efficient delivery of monthly results reporting for strategic initiatives like Offer personalization, Orchestra
  • Support and enable the business partners with campaign measurements, ROI analysis and actionable data driven insights for acquisition strategy in GCS
  • Keen on exploration and analysis of data from various data sources, translate the same to excellent visual case studies.
  • Quick learner quick to understand the business and support pilot projects amidst ambiguity
  • Collaborate with functional leaders, business partners and senior leaders to assess business needs, evaluate project feasibility, ensure data integrity, and provide actionable recommendations in times of constant change
  • Build a solid relationship/partnership with Marketing Stakeholders, drive projects end to end with full accountability and growth mindset

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Knowledge of Hive, Python, PySpark, SQL encompassing data manipulation and statistical modeling/data-mining techniques
  • Strong analytical aptitude and technical skills
  • Project Management skills
  • Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal communications skills with an ability to formulate and communicate results & methodologies in a clear and compelling way
  • A demonstrated ability to work in high-pressure situations with minimal errors
  • An optimistic and growth mind-set with a tremendous will to win
  • Should be able to function with minimum supervision with high sense of ownership
  • Strong technical and problem-solving skills
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to analyze large amounts of data and create innovative solutions
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment
  • Ability to learn quickly and work independently with complex, unstructured initiatives
  • Ability to challenge the status quo and drive innovation

Preferred Qualification

  • Degree in a quantitative field preferred – e.g. B. Tech; BSc/MSc Statistics, Bachelors/master’s in economics or equivalent work experience
  • Experience in marketing analytics and campaign measurement preferred; relevant industry experience preferred
  • 1+ years of relevant experience with strong knowledge of skills like Python, SQL & Advanced Excel required; R also good to have.
  • Good knowledge of statistical techniques like hypothesis testing, regression, XGBoost, KNN, t-test, chi-square test

Data Analyst – Operations (Restaurant & Catering)

The Data Analyst at CAVA provides analytical support by preparing data, delivering insights, and creating dashboards that inform strategic reporting requirements while supporting short-term ad-hoc needs. This role partners closely with Data Product Managers to ensure data accuracy, usability, and alignment with enterprise standards.




Data Analysts play a critical role in transforming raw data into actionable insights, enabling business teams to make informed decisions and optimize performance.




*This role is part of the Data Product Team supporting our Restaurant & Catering Operation*




Success at CAVA: At CAVA, Individual Contributors focus on delivering high-quality work, owning tasks, and contributing to team success. We look for team members who take initiative, collaborate across teams, and stay curious about how their work supports broader goals.




What You’ll Do:

Analytics & Reporting

Query, clean, and transform datasets to support reporting and analysis.
Validate data accuracy for new metrics, dashboards, and analytical assets.
Support ad-hoc analytics requests and long-term strategic reporting needs.

Visualization & UX

Create interactive dashboards and reports aligned with business needs and UX best practices.
Ensure consistency in branding using enterprise visualization templates.
Optimize dashboards for scalability, usability, and performance.

Collaboration & Governance

Work closely with Data Product Managers as domain SMEs to understand requirements.
Partner with Data Engineers to align on logic and data source integration.
Define and implement data quality checks to ensure compliance with governance standards.
Collaborate with product owners to iterate designs and improve user experience.

The Qualifications:

Required

2–5 years of experience in data analysis or business intelligence.
Proficiency in SQL (or python) and experience with data querying and transformation.
Strong skills in data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
Ability to work with structured and semi-structured data.
Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

Preferred

Experience with Python or R for data manipulation and analysis.
Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks).
Understanding of data governance and quality assurance practices.

Physical Requirements:

Ability to maintain stationary position to be able to operate a computer and other office equipment
Must be able to identify, analyze and assess details
For certain positions, must be able to occasionally move or transport items up to 50 pounds
Ability to communicate with others and exchange information accurately and effectively
Constantly positions self and move about to support ordinary restaurant or food production support or office operations, as applicable
Ability to work in a constant state of alertness and in a safe manner

What we offer:

Competitive salary, plus bonus and long-term incentives*
Early Wage Access!
Unlimited PTO, paid parental leave, plus paid opportunities to give back to the community
Health, Dental, Vision, Telemedicine, Pet Insurance plus more!
401k enrollment with CAVA contribution
Company-paid STD, LTD, Life and AD&D coverage for salaried positions*
Free CAVA food
Casual work environment
The opportunity to be on the ground floor of a rapidly growing brand

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are BLG: Canada’s Law Firm. Our culture is one of vigilance and curiosity, Firm-wide collaboration, unfailing mutual respect and dedication. We pride ourselves on having standout talent and delivering an exceptional client experience.







We are a future-minded Firm delivering high-value advice and known for our unwavering commitment to innovation, diversity and inclusion, community involvement and talent development. Learn more about us at www.blg.com.




Purpose of Role




This is a full-time, 6 month contract opportunity




The Analyst, Data Management will report to the Senior Manager, Data Management and will contribute to the development and execution of plans, policies, and practices that control, protect, deliver and enhance the value of BLG’s data and information assets.







This role will be responsible for assisting in the implementation of enterprise data strategy, initiation and successful execution of strategic data management projects and continuous improvement initiatives.




Key Responsibilities




The Analyst, Data Management will be responsible for maintaining data definitions, establishing data quality standards, monitoring compliance to those standards, improving data quality, assist in the development of master/reference data and business intelligence/analytical solutions.




The role will contribute to the implementation of data warehouse platform using Microsoft Fabric, with a focus on centralization and the protection of sensitive data through creation of appropriate organization policies, standards, principles, governance metrics, processes, related tools and data architecture.




The incumbent will be required to work with diverse stakeholders/data owners to understand current and future data needs and work with Senior Manager, Data Management to assist with the development of roadmaps (tactical and strategic).




Assist with implementing a data management and governance framework aligned with BLG’s business objectives.
Assist with the implementation of data catalog solution, to hold firm-wide technical metadata and data glossary
Assist with the historical data quality/cleanup initiatives and develop rules to validate the data across different enterprise systems.
Work with sponsors and stakeholders to implement processes, policies, firm-wide business data definitions, data quality rules, domains, validation and resolution policies and method that will support a successful data management program.
Implement master/reference data management standards, enforce data governance procedures and ensure data integrity across multiple platforms to ensure proper use of data.
Establish monitoring and reporting processes to ensure high standards of data quality and conformance with data policies, procedures and standards.
Develop indicators of performance, quality metrics and ensure compliance with data related policies, standards, roles and responsibilities.
Develop & maintain inventory of the enterprise data sources, including authoritative systems and owners.
Provide recommendations on the development of data management policies, procedures and standards to ensure best practices of data management. Assist with the formulation of new guidance and standards.
Analyze business processes and identify data and information requirements (i.e. what information the business process requires, produces, collects and stores; who needs access to it; for what purpose and for how long). Recommend improvements in business processes as appropriate.
Ensure compliance with roles and responsibilities related to data governance and ensure clear data ownership and stewardship of the BLGs data assets.
Participate in Data Governance Council meetings, comprising of Data Owners, communicate decisions, plans, goals and strategies related to enterprise data.
Coordinate with data stewards and data owners across the enterprise to address data quality issues.
Collaborate with centralized analytics team to deliver business intelligence solutions
Maintain best practice guidelines and standards for data, metadata, data modelling, and management.
Investigate tools and technologies to assist with data management and dissemination, such as data warehouse and analytics tools, data integration tools, data quality and profiling tools, outlining risks, costs, benefits, and capabilities.
Responsible for maintaining and enhancing the data quality scorecards




Key Competencies

Experience implementing best practices, adoption methodologies and standards with regards to Data Analytics, Data Governance, Data Structure, Data Architecture, and Data Security.
Knowledgeable in data profiling, root cause analysis, and quality improvement.
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric, Python notebooks, Data Pipeline, Data Factory is an asset.
Knowledge of DAMA framework is an asset.
Well versed in the following data domains: Master/Reference Data, Operational Data, Analytical Data, Unstructured Data, and Metadata.
Solid understanding of transactional and information systems, data warehouses, data marts, MDM methodologies and life cycles.
Hands-on experience with data analysis, quality, cleaning and extraction / validation tools.
Knowledge of Reporting tools like SSRS, Power BI is an asset.
Strong proficiency with SQL-based languages. Ability to write and troubleshoot complex SQL queries.
Hands-on experience with implementation of a data catalog/dictionary.
Minimum 2 years of experience in Data Management design principles, concepts and implementing data-centric solutions.
Minimum 2 years of experience with BI methodology and technologies.
Knowledge of Informatica suite of tools is an asset.
Ability to translate user requirements into technical specifications and systems.
Understanding the IT architecture discipline, processes, concepts, and best practices.




The expected salary range for this position is $90,000 - $110,000, depending on level of experience, region/location, and other factors.

BLG is committed to building a diverse workplace reflective of the communities we serve and to fostering an inclusive culture where Firm members feel valued, respected and inspired to thrive as their authentic selves. We value diversity of thought and the unique skills, perspectives and experience each individual brings to BLG. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates and encourage applicants from members of groups that have been historically underrepresented, including but not limited to First Nations, Métis and Inuit Peoples, racialized individuals, persons with disabilities, people who identify as women and/or LGBTQ2S+.

  • Work closely with Data Product Managers as domain SMEs to understand requirements.
  • Partner with Data Engineers to align on logic and data source integration.
  • Define and implement data quality checks to ensure compliance with governance standards.
  • Collaborate with product owners to iterate designs and improve user experience.

Required

The Qualifications:

  • 2–5 years of experience in data analysis or business intelligence.
  • Proficiency in SQL (or python) and experience with data querying and transformation.
  • Strong skills in data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
  • Ability to work with structured and semi-structured data.
  • Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

Preferred

  • Experience with Python or R for data manipulation and analysis.
  • Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks).
  • Understanding of data governance and quality assurance practices.

Physical Requirements

  • Ability to maintain stationary position to be able to operate a computer and other office equipment
  • Must be able to identify, analyze and assess details
  • For certain positions, must be able to occasionally move or transport items up to 50 pounds
  • Ability to communicate with others and exchange information accurately and effectively
  • Constantly positions self and move about to support ordinary restaurant or food production support or office operations, as applicable
  • Ability to work in a constant state of alertness and in a safe manner

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary, plus bonus and long-term incentives*
  • Early Wage Access!
  • Unlimited PTO, paid parental leave, plus paid opportunities to give back to the community
  • Health, Dental, Vision, Telemedicine, Pet Insurance plus more!
  • 401k enrollment with CAVA contribution
  • Company-paid STD, LTD, Life and AD&D coverage for salaried positions*
  • Free CAVA food
