Title: Analista de datos
Description:
Overview

CARRERAS PARA SONREÍR

En PepsiCo, descubrirás un lugar donde nuestra misión es crear sonrisas alrededor del mundo. Con más de 500 marcas queridas, incluyen Gatorade, Lay's y Quaker y nuestro trabajo llega a millones de personas cada día.

 

En el corazón de nuestra compañía hay un equipo de pensadores y creadores que colaboran para innovar y convertir las ideas en acciones. Impulsados por la innovación y centrados en crear momentos de alegría a través de los alimentos y bebidas, nuestras decisiones se guían por y para el consumidor, creando oportunidades para que nuestros asociados realicen un trabajo significativo y tengan un impacto duradero en las comunidades a las que servimos.

 

Sea cual sea tu función, formarás parte de una comunidad global que valora tus ideas y te empodera para que tengas impacto en tu carrera y en el mundo que te rodea.

Responsibilities

La oportunidad

El Analista de Datos con Enfoque en Servicio de Ventas es responsable de recopilar, procesar y analizar información clave para apoyar la toma de decisiones comerciales. Su función combina habilidades analíticas con atención al cliente interno brindando soporte operativo al área de ventas mediante reportes, seguimiento de indicadores y gestión básica de documentación utilizando herramientas del paquete Office.

Tu impacto


Recopilar, depurar y analizar datos provenientes de diversas fuentes para generar reportes comerciales y operativos.
Elaborar dashboards e indicadores clave de desempeño (KPIs) que faciliten el monitoreo de resultados del área de ventas.
Brindar soporte directo al equipo comercial en requerimientos de información, cotizaciones, seguimiento a clientes (alta, bajas y Modificaciones)
Mantener actualizadas bases de datos de clientes, productos, ventas y oportunidades. Mantener un sano inventario de Dispositivos celulares y Impresoras
Preparar informes periódicos en Excel, PowerPoint y Word para presentaciones internas.
Detectar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora en el comportamiento de ventas.
Dar seguimiento a solicitudes de clientes y coordinar la comunicación con áreas involucradas.
Apoyar en la automatización o simplificación de procesos mediante herramientas básicas del paquete Office.
Conocimientos y Habilidades Análisis de datos: manejo de bases de datos, interpretación de información, análisis estadístico básico, elaboración de reportes. Ventas y servicio al cliente: comunicación efectiva, seguimiento de clientes, comprensión del ciclo comercial. Paquete Office (nivel básico–intermedio): Excel: tablas, filtros, gráficos, fórmulas básicas. PowerPoint: elaboración de presentaciones ejecutivas. Word: redacción y formateo de documentos. Pensamiento crítico orientado a resultados. Organización, atención al detalle y control documental. Capacidad para trabajar bajo objetivos y múltiples solicitudes simultáneas.


Qualifications

¿A quién buscamos?


Formación académica en Administración, Ingeniería, Negocios, Mercadotecnia, Sistemas o afines.
Experiencia en análisis de datos, soporte comercial o servicio al cliente(1 año)
Habilidad para trabajar en equipo, resolver problemas y mantener una comunicación clara con áreas comerciales.
Paquetería office básico
Disponibilidad para laborar L-S (turnos rotativos matutino y despertino)


Si esta es una oportunidad que te interesa, te alentamos a postularte aún si no cumples con el 100% de los requisitos.

Qué puedes esperar de nosotros:


Oportunidades para aprender y desarrollarte todos los días a través de una amplia oferta de programas.
Plataformas internas digitales que promueven el self-learning.
Programas de desarrollo de acuerdo con habilidades de Liderazgo.
Entrenamientos especializados de acuerdo con el rol.
Experiencias de aprendizaje con proveedores internos y externos.
Nos encanta celebrar el éxito, por lo que contamos con programas de reconocimiento por antigüedad, comportamientos, liderazgo, momentos de vida, entre otros.
Programas de bienestar financiero que te ayudarán a alcanzar tus metas en todas las etapas de la vida.
Un programa de flexibilidad que te permitirá balancear tu vida personal y laboral, adaptando tu jornada laboral a tu estilo de vida.
Y porque tu familia también es importante para nosotros, ellos también pueden gozar de beneficios como nuestra Línea de Bienestar, miles de Convenios y Descuentos, programas de Becas para tus hijos, Planes de Ayuda para diferentes momentos de vida, entre otros.


En PepsiCo, estamos comprometidos con impulsar un equipo de trabajo diverso al crear un espacio colaborativo, equitativo e incluyente, en donde todos y todas independientemente de cómo nos vemos, de dónde somos o a quién amamos- tengamos una voz.

Ofrecemos oportunidades únicas para contratar personas calificadas y diversas, independientemente de su género, raza, orientación sexual, religión, nacionalidad, edad o discapacidad.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
NOTE: Candidates must be based in the Los Angeles Metropolitan area.




We are seeking a Data Analyst to support Zelig, an AI-powered virtual try-on and styling platform redefining how consumers shop. This role is focused on interpreting complex data, including outputs from data models, to understand how system decisions impact business outcomes and to optimize lead-flow and decisioning processes.




This is a highly analytical, business-focused role. While dashboards and visualizations are important, the primary responsibility is data interpretation, decision analysis, and optimization, rather than heavy engineering work.




Zelig is a high-growth company building best-in-class technology, and we are looking for someone who is quick on their feet, naturally curious, and thrives in a collaborative, fast-paced environment.




Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Business Impact

Interpret complex datasets to understand how system decisions impact business outcomes
Tie Zelig recommendations to measurable KPIs such as conversion, funnel progression, and efficiency
Identify opportunities to optimize lead flow logic and decision rules to improve performance
Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable business recommendations




Data Exploration & Analysis

Extract and analyze data from reports and datasets using Excel or Google Sheets
Develop a deep understanding of data sources, reporting tools, and inherent limitations
Validate assumptions and investigate anomalies across systems and outputs
Manage and synthesize multiple datasets across partners, building both internal and client-facing insights




Product & Stakeholder Collaboration

Partner closely with Product to define requirements, success metrics, and experimentation frameworks
Collaborate on Proofs of Concept (POCs), including stitching and combining data across multiple sources
Help document metrics, business logic, and data definitions to ensure consistency and alignment




Enablement & Communication

Communicate insights effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Tailor storytelling based on audience (executive vs. operational)
Support UX and design partners by providing data context for dashboards and reporting experiences




Team Environment

Work cross-functionally with Product, Analytics, and external data partners (including Puller)
Collaborate with UX designers to shape intuitive and impactful data visualizations
Contribute as a strong team player in a fast-moving, high-growth environment




Must-Have Qualifications

Strong working knowledge of SQL. Demonstrated ability to write, review, and optimize complex SQL — including the judgment to evaluate query logic and improve performance.
Experience with Tableau and BigQuery (or similar BI, data warehouse tools).
3–5 years of experience in a Product, Data, or Analytics role, preferably within the software or technology sector
Strong data literacy with the ability to assess data quality, logic, and business impact
Proven ability to translate analysis into actionable recommendations
Extensive experience using data to inform product or business decisions
Comfortable working with reports, metrics, and exploratory analysis
Strong storytelling skills with data and experience presenting insights clearly
Ability to adapt communication style for different audiences (executive vs. operational)

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support Zelig, an AI-powered virtual try-on and styling platform redefining how consumers shop. This role is focused on interpreting complex data, including outputs from data models, to understand how system decisions impact business outcomes and to optimize lead-flow and decisioning processes.


This is a highly analytical, business-focused role. While dashboards and visualizations are important, the primary responsibility is data interpretation, decision analysis, and optimization, rather than heavy engineering work.


Zelig is a high-growth company building best-in-class technology, and we are looking for someone who is quick on their feet, naturally curious, and thrives in a collaborative, fast-paced environment.


Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Business Impact

  • Interpret complex datasets to understand how system decisions impact business outcomes
  • Tie Zelig recommendations to measurable KPIs such as conversion, funnel progression, and efficiency
  • Identify opportunities to optimize lead flow logic and decision rules to improve performance
  • Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable business recommendations


Data Exploration & Analysis

  • Extract and analyze data from reports and datasets using Excel or Google Sheets
  • Develop a deep understanding of data sources, reporting tools, and inherent limitations
  • Validate assumptions and investigate anomalies across systems and outputs
  • Manage and synthesize multiple datasets across partners, building both internal and client-facing insights


Product & Stakeholder Collaboration

  • Partner closely with Product to define requirements, success metrics, and experimentation frameworks
  • Collaborate on Proofs of Concept (POCs), including stitching and combining data across multiple sources
  • Help document metrics, business logic, and data definitions to ensure consistency and alignment


Enablement & Communication

  • Communicate insights effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Tailor storytelling based on audience (executive vs. operational)
  • Support UX and design partners by providing data context for dashboards and reporting experiences


Team Environment

  • Work cross-functionally with Product, Analytics, and external data partners (including Puller)
  • Collaborate with UX designers to shape intuitive and impactful data visualizations
  • Contribute as a strong team player in a fast-moving, high-growth environment


Must-Have Qualifications

  • Strong working knowledge of SQL. Demonstrated ability to write, review, and optimize complex SQL — including the judgment to evaluate query logic and improve performance.
  • Experience with Tableau and BigQuery (or similar BI, data warehouse tools).
  • 3–5 years of experience in a Product, Data, or Analytics role, preferably within the software or technology sector
  • Strong data literacy with the ability to assess data quality, logic, and business impact
  • Proven ability to translate analysis into actionable recommendations
  • Extensive experience using data to inform product or business decisions
  • Comfortable working with reports, metrics, and exploratory analysis
  • Strong storytelling skills with data and experience presenting insights clearly
  • Ability to adapt communication style for different audiences (executive vs. operational)
  • Experience with experimentation, optimization, or funnel analysis
  • Exposure to data science-driven or rules-based decisioning systems
  • Ability to manage and synthesize multiple datasets across internal and external partners
  • Experience working in or alongside AI-driven products is a strong plus

  • Quick thinker who can operate effectively in a fast-paced, evolving environment
  • Strong collaborator who works well across teams and disciplines
  • Someone that can work in the office 4-5 days a week

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
Responsibilities

Kforce has a client that is seeking a Junior Data Analyst I (Dashboard Developer) in Englewood, CO. About the Role: We are looking for a sharp, curious Junior BA to join our team building and maintain operational dashboards in Grafana. You will be working on dashboards that support the deployment and monitoring of Distributed Access Architecture (DAA) infrastructure - specifically RPD activation workflows and streaming telemetry from vCMTS devices. This is a hands-on role focused on data visualization, query writing, and dashboard design. You will work closely with a senior lead who will mentor you, help you ramp up on the domain, and gradually grow your exposure to stakeholders and customers. You don't need to know everything on day one - but you do need to be eager to learn and comfortable working with structured data. Duties:


Build, maintain, and improve Grafana dashboards used by operations and engineering teams
Write and optimize queries against MongoDB (aggregation pipelines) and SQL-based data stores (StarTree Pinot)
Translate business requirements into clear, usable dashboard visualizations
Troubleshoot data issues and dashboard behavior
Advise on data structure improvements to support better reporting (you won't code the changes, but your input matters)
Document dashboard designs, query logic, and data models


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related field or equivalent practical experience
0-2 years of relevant experience (internships, academic projects, and personal projects count)


Technical


Foundational understanding of computer science concepts (data structures, logic, how systems connect)
Experience writing SQL or a similar language (academic or professional)
Comfort working with structured data formats (JSON, CSV)
Basic understanding of databases - what a query does, how data is organized, how joins work
Ability to read and reason about code, even if you're not writing production software daily


Soft Skills


Strong problem-solving instinct - you like figuring things out
Intellectual curiosity - you ask "why" and dig until you understand
Clear communicator - you can explain what you built and why
Coachable - you take feedback well and apply it quickly
Detail-oriented - dashboards are only useful if the data is right


Nice-to-Have Skills:


Exposure to Grafana or similar dashboarding/BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, etc.)
Familiarity with MongoDB or NoSQL databases
Familiarity with time-series data concepts or streaming data (Kafka, PromQL)
Exposure to JSON manipulation or data transformation scripting (Python, JS, etc.)
Comfort working in a telecom or infrastructure operations environment
Interest in network technology or cable/broadband systems
Experience (even informal) presenting data or insights to non-technical audiences


The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. Employee pay is based on factors like relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, location, performance, union contract and business needs. This range may be modified in the future.

We offer comprehensive benefits including medical/dental/vision insurance, HSA, FSA, 401(k), and life, disability & ADD insurance to eligible employees. Salaried personnel receive paid time off. Hourly employees are not eligible for paid time off unless required by law. Hourly employees on a Service Contract Act project are eligible for paid sick leave.

Note: Pay is not considered compensation until it is earned, vested and determinable. The amount and availability of any compensation remains in Kforce's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified in its discretion consistent with the law.

This job is not eligible for bonuses, incentives or commissions.

Kforce is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, protected veteran status, or disability status.

By clicking “Apply Today” you agree to receive calls, AI-generated calls, text messages or emails from Kforce and its affiliates, and service providers. Note that if you choose to communicate with Kforce via text messaging the frequency may vary, and message and data rates may apply. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. You will always have the right to cease communicating via text by using key words such as STOP.

Title: BI Data Analyst
Description:
About Us:

We are a leading provider of ERP software and solutions catering to dealers and rental companies in the heavy equipment sector. With a focus on industries like construction, mining, forestry, material handling, and agriculture, we aspire to be the forefront thought leader in ERP solutions, serving as a trusted advisor to our clientele. Operating globally with over 700 employees across four continents, we are committed to expanding our North American presence with both on-premises and cloud-based ERP solutions from our headquarters in Cary, NC.




Why Join VitalEdge?

At VitalEdge, we're not just about technology; we're about driving transformative change that positively impacts society, from building homes and critical infrastructure to facilitating food production and product delivery. Our mission is to empower the world to keep moving forward. With over 60 years of combined industry experience and two leading software suites, we are poised to drive innovation and progress. Join us in this exciting journey!




About the role:

Lead and manage client-facing BI Analytics & Insights engagements for Heavy Equipment Dealership groups across Construction, Agriculture, and Material Handling. This is a senior consulting role responsible for helping dealership executives modernize analytics, standardize KPIs, and translate operational data into measurable financial and operational outcomes.




The role blends deep dealership operations knowledge, advanced Microsoft BI capabilities, and executive advisory skills. Partner directly with dealer principals, CFOs, and functional leaders to design scalable KPI frameworks, modernize reporting, and drive data-led performance improvement across multi-branch dealer organizations.




Responsibilities:

Client Advisory & Engagement

Own client analytics roadmap from discovery through value realization
Lead discovery workshops with dealer principals
Analyze requirements, diagnose gaps across departmental operations
Translate dealership strategy into measurable KPI frameworks
Present executive-ready insights and KPI roadmaps
Manage multiple customer engagements concurrently




Analytics Solution Design & Delivery

Define / Analyze business requirements, metrics, and scoping for development and /or customization of reports/dashboards/KPIs
Architect and standardize operational dashboards for dealerships based on roles, industry, and multi-branch/multi-geographical dealer groups
Improve reporting governance, automation, and data integrity




Dealership Performance Optimization

Drive measurable improvement across industry metrics and KPIs such as (but not limited to):
Service absorption rates
Labor Productivity
Parts inventory turns
Equipment Sales margin and ROA
Rental fleet utilization and ROI




Practice & Business Development

Support pre-sales efforts through solution design and customer presentations
Contribute to proposal development and scoping
Position VitalEdge as a thought leader in heavy equipment industry metrics and analytics




Required Experience

6+ years in Business Intelligence, Analytics Consulting
Strong experience in Heavy Equipment dealership environments across Construction, Material Handling, Agriculture, or Mining, including direct exposure to dealership financial and operational KPIs.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft BI (Power BI, Semantic Models, ETLs, Fabric, Data Lake)
Strong SQL and data modelling expertise
Proven ability to influence senior operational leaders




Preferred Qualifications

Microsoft Certified Power BI Data Analyst

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
Stackline is the first full-funnel connected commerce platform for the world's most innovative brands. Business leaders, product innovators, performance marketers, and analysts trust Stackline as the single source of commerce truth. Fueled by our proprietary neural network, our market insights, revenue metrics, behavior analyses, and autonomous functionality create the actions that determine success or failure.

Founded in 2014 in Seattle, we have offices in Minneapolis, New York, Salt Lake City, and London. Since November 2020, Stackline has raised $180 million in strategic investments from Goldman Sachs Growth Equity and TA Associates.

Stackline is on a mission to fuel the future of commerce by bringing brands and customers closer together.

About the Role:

Join us as a Data Analyst where you will step into a dynamic role that that makes a real impact for our clients. If you are passionate about uncovering hidden insights within complex data sets and transforming raw data into actionable knowledge, then we want to talk with you! In this role you will embark on an adventure that utilized your analytical prowess to decode data, identify trends, and help shape the future of Stackline. Apply today and help us on our quest to unlock the full potential of data while contributing to meaningful change! This is a hybrid role (4 days/week in office) and is based out of our Seattle, WA office.

What You Will Do:


Conduct comprehensive dataset analyses across Stackline to extract insights, trends, and patterns utilizing mathematical, statistical, and probability methodologies.
Build and maintain data pipelines and workflows to ensure data accuracy and consistency, while identifying and rectifying errors, inconsistencies, or missing information.
Provide timely and precise responses to inquiries from both internal and external sources regarding datasets and models.
Apply technical expertise to process complex datasets, establish quality control procedures, and derive actionable insights through analytics.
Develop scalable processes for automating reports and visualizations to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and address specific product questions or challenges.
Document data sources and processes for data analysis.


Who We Are Looking For:


Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, or other relevant technical field.
2+ years of direct industry work experience in one or more of the following areas: data science, data analytics, or data engineering.
2+ years of experience with statistics and probability, particularly their application in systems analysis and operations research.
Demonstrated experience with Python and SQL.


Bonus Points If You Have:


Prior experience using advanced statistical tools such as SciPy or sklearn.
Prior experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop or Spark.
Demonstrated experience designing and building innovative ideas, working closely with technical teams from concept generation through implementation.
Experience working in a startup, retail, digital advertising, or e-commerce environment.


Benefits And Perks

It's important that each and every employee feels they are supported and can complete their life's best work today and in the future. As part of that, we are committed to doing our part in addressing pay gaps and discrepancies by providing pay transparency for all of our roles. Actual salaries are just one component of the compensation package and may vary above or below the range based on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, location, and performance. The pay range for this position in Seattle is $85,000 - $105,000 per year. Other rewards may include annual bonuses, short- and long-term incentives, and other team-specific awards. In addition we provide a robust benefits and perks package that includes:


Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage that actually supports you — including HSA with company match and FSA options
Fertility benefits to support your path to parenthood
401(k) with company match to help you plan ahead
Company-paid life insurance
20 days of PTO + 9 company holidays to truly unplug
Paid parental leave for all parents — because family matters
Summer Fridays (log off at 3pm and enjoy it)
Regular in-office social events including happy hours and catered lunches
A thoughtfully stocked kitchen with healthy snacks and fresh fruit


Stackline is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

Title: Data Analyst – Operations: Washington, DC
Description:
At CAVA we make it deliciously simple to eat well and feel good every day. We are guided by a Mediterranean heritage that’s been perfecting how to eat and live for four thousand years. We prioritize authenticity, curiosity, and the pursuit of excellence in everything we do. We are working towards something big together.

We foster a culture built on five core values:

Generosity First, Always: We lead with kindness. Our best work happens when we act in service of others.
Constant Curiosity: We are eager to learn, grow, and explore beyond the obvious.
Act with Agility: We welcome change; it’s the only constant. We embrace, adjust, adapt.
Passion for Positivity: We greet each day with warmth and possibility.
Collective Ambition: We have high aspirations that are achieved when we work together with a shared purpose.

Data Analyst – Operations (Restaurant & Catering)

The Data Analyst at CAVA provides analytical support by preparing data, delivering insights, and creating dashboards that inform strategic reporting requirements while supporting short-term ad-hoc needs. This role partners closely with Data Product Managers to ensure data accuracy, usability, and alignment with enterprise standards.




Data Analysts play a critical role in transforming raw data into actionable insights, enabling business teams to make informed decisions and optimize performance.




*This role is part of the Data Product Team supporting our Restaurant & Catering Operation*




Success at CAVA: At CAVA, Individual Contributors focus on delivering high-quality work, owning tasks, and contributing to team success. We look for team members who take initiative, collaborate across teams, and stay curious about how their work supports broader goals.




What You’ll Do:

Analytics & Reporting

Query, clean, and transform datasets to support reporting and analysis.
Validate data accuracy for new metrics, dashboards, and analytical assets.
Support ad-hoc analytics requests and long-term strategic reporting needs.

Visualization & UX

Create interactive dashboards and reports aligned with business needs and UX best practices.
Ensure consistency in branding using enterprise visualization templates.
Optimize dashboards for scalability, usability, and performance.

Collaboration & Governance

Work closely with Data Product Managers as domain SMEs to understand requirements.
Partner with Data Engineers to align on logic and data source integration.
Define and implement data quality checks to ensure compliance with governance standards.
Collaborate with product owners to iterate designs and improve user experience.

The Qualifications:

Required

2–5 years of experience in data analysis or business intelligence.
Proficiency in SQL (or python) and experience with data querying and transformation.
Strong skills in data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
Ability to work with structured and semi-structured data.
Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

Preferred

Experience with Python or R for data manipulation and analysis.
Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks).
Understanding of data governance and quality assurance practices.

Physical Requirements:

Ability to maintain stationary position to be able to operate a computer and other office equipment
Must be able to identify, analyze and assess details
For certain positions, must be able to occasionally move or transport items up to 50 pounds
Ability to communicate with others and exchange information accurately and effectively
Constantly positions self and move about to support ordinary restaurant or food production support or office operations, as applicable
Ability to work in a constant state of alertness and in a safe manner

What we offer:

Competitive salary, plus bonus and long-term incentives*
Early Wage Access!
Unlimited PTO, paid parental leave, plus paid opportunities to give back to the community
Health, Dental, Vision, Telemedicine, Pet Insurance plus more!
401k enrollment with CAVA contribution
Company-paid STD, LTD, Life and AD&D coverage for salaried positions*
Free CAVA food
Casual work environment
The opportunity to be on the ground floor of a rapidly growing brand
All exempt and non exempt employees are eligible for benefits. Benefits are effective the 1st of the month following 30 days of service and you have until the day before the effective date to enroll. A new hire can enroll in our benefit program by selecting a link that is emailed directly to the new hire at their personal email address once hired.
Please note that visa sponsorship is not available.
The posting range is an all cash range.







*Indicates qualifying eligible positions only




CAVA – Joining “A culture, not a concept”




As an equal opportunity employer, CAVA considers applicants for all positions without regard to race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, age, height, weight, marital status, sexual orientation, familial status, genetic information or any other characteristic or protected classes as defined by federal, state, or local law.

California applicants: Please visit our Notice at Collection and Privacy Policy to learn about our information practices in the job application and employment context.

At CAVA we make it deliciously simple to eat well and feel good every day. We are guided by a Mediterranean heritage that’s been perfecting how to eat and live for four thousand years. We prioritize authenticity, curiosity, and the pursuit of excellence in everything we do. We are working towards something big together.

We foster a culture built on five core values:

  • Generosity First, Always: We lead with kindness. Our best work happens when we act in service of others.
  • Constant Curiosity: We are eager to learn, grow, and explore beyond the obvious.
  • Act with Agility: We welcome change; it’s the only constant. We embrace, adjust, adapt.
  • Passion for Positivity: We greet each day with warmth and possibility.
  • Collective Ambition: We have high aspirations that are achieved when we work together with a shared purpose.

Data Analyst – Operations (Restaurant & Catering)

The Data Analyst at CAVA provides analytical support by preparing data, delivering insights, and creating dashboards that inform strategic reporting requirements while supporting short-term ad-hoc needs. This role partners closely with Data Product Managers to ensure data accuracy, usability, and alignment with enterprise standards.


Data Analysts play a critical role in transforming raw data into actionable insights, enabling business teams to make informed decisions and optimize performance.


*This role is part of the Data Product Team supporting our Restaurant & Catering Operation*


Success at CAVA: At CAVA, Individual Contributors focus on delivering high-quality work, owning tasks, and contributing to team success. We look for team members who take initiative, collaborate across teams, and stay curious about how their work supports broader goals.


What You’ll Do:

Analytics & Reporting

  • Query, clean, and transform datasets to support reporting and analysis.
  • Validate data accuracy for new metrics, dashboards, and analytical assets.
  • Support ad-hoc analytics requests and long-term strategic reporting needs.

Visualization & UX

  • Create interactive dashboards and reports aligned with business needs and UX best practices.
  • Ensure consistency in branding using enterprise visualization templates.
  • Optimize dashboards for scalability, usability, and performance.

Collaboration & Governance

  • Work closely with Data Product Managers as domain SMEs to understand requirements.
  • Partner with Data Engineers to align on logic and data source integration.
  • Define and implement data quality checks to ensure compliance with governance standards.
  • Collaborate with product owners to iterate designs and improve user experience.

The Qualifications:

Required

  • 2–5 years of experience in data analysis or business intelligence.
  • Proficiency in SQL (or python) and experience with data querying and transformation.
  • Strong skills in data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
  • Ability to work with structured and semi-structured data.
  • Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

Preferred

  • Experience with Python or R for data manipulation and analysis.
  • Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks).
  • Understanding of data governance and quality assurance practices.

Physical Requirements:

  • Ability to maintain stationary position to be able to operate a computer and other office equipment
  • Must be able to identify, analyze and assess details
  • For certain positions, must be able to occasionally move or transport items up to 50 pounds
  • Ability to communicate with others and exchange information accurately and effectively
  • Constantly positions self and move about to support ordinary restaurant or food production support or office operations, as applicable
  • Ability to work in a constant state of alertness and in a safe manner

What we offer:

  • Competitive salary, plus bonus and long-term incentives*
  • Early Wage Access!
  • Unlimited PTO, paid parental leave, plus paid opportunities to give back to the community
  • Health, Dental, Vision, Telemedicine, Pet Insurance plus more!
  • 401k enrollment with CAVA contribution
  • Company-paid STD, LTD, Life and AD&D coverage for salaried positions*
  • Free CAVA food
  • Casual work environment
  • The opportunity to be on the ground floor of a rapidly growing brand
  • All exempt and non exempt employees are eligible for benefits. Benefits are effective the 1st of the month following 30 days of service and you have until the day before the effective date to enroll. A new hire can enroll in our benefit program by selecting a link that is emailed directly to the new hire at their personal email address once hired.
  • Please note that visa sponsorship is not available.
  • The posting range is an all cash range.



*Indicates qualifying eligible positions only


CAVA – Joining “A culture, not a concept”


As an equal opportunity employer, CAVA considers applicants for all positions without regard to race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, age, height, weight, marital status, sexual orientation, familial status, genetic information or any other characteristic or protected classes as defined by federal, state, or local law.

California applicants: Please visit our Notice at Collection and Privacy Policy to learn about our information practices in the job application and employment context.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, validate, and organize data from multiple internal and external sources
Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, anomalies, and actionable business insights
Design and develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, and Excel
Work closely with business, product, operations, and technical teams to understand reporting and analytics requirements
Monitor key business KPIs and prepare regular performance and operational reports
Perform ad-hoc data analysis to support business decisions and strategic initiatives
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, integrity, and proper documentation across reporting systems
Present analytical findings and recommendations clearly to stakeholders and leadership teams
Assist in improving reporting automation and analytics processes
Support data governance and best practices related to reporting and analytics

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capabilities
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to present complex data insights in a simple and business-friendly manner
Strong attention to detail and ability to work in a fast-paced environment

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
What’s The Role

Shell Canada is seeking a Data Analyst (also known internally as an Operations Analyst) to join its team at the Scotford Refinery.

This role provides essential analytical support for Scotford, including Production. This role reports to the Operations Central Services Team and is responsible for effective data monitoring and tracking of critical business functions. These include budget management, overtime control, execution efficiency, and monitoring key performance indicators. Together, these activities are fundamental in driving improvements in overall business performance.

Serving as a key enabler for the interpretation of site-wide data at Scotford, the Data Analyst also supports the Site Leadership Team through the Operations Analyst Lead. The role involves consolidating data from multiple sources, disciplines, and departments to accurately assess where business targets are being met and to identify areas requiring corrective action. The successful candidate is also responsible for clearly communicating insights to stakeholders, supporting Scotford’s continuous improvement journey and alignment with its business objectives.

Accountabilities:


Deliver comprehensive analysis for the Production team to provide the organization with assurance on key performance metrics
Prepare monthly reports to assess and deliver performance insights e.g. budget report, maintenance efficiency
Verify and ensure integrity of data and data sources in dashboards and reports
Transform raw data into actionable information by leveraging tools such as Power BI, Excel, and PowerPoint
Support HR ER/IR teams by performing data analysis as required
Provide data analyst support to key functions e.g. Maintenance and Engineering
Provide oversight and analytical review of data within SchedulePro
Analyze the application of the Hourly Pay Practice (HPP), Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA), and scheduling practices.
Communicate key insights to leaders to ensure consistent application of policies and practices
Offer time entry and backup support for the Production Time Focals regarding payroll entry
Provide executive and administrative support to Production and other departments
Serve as backup support to the Operations Analyst Lead when needed
May be required to fill a reserve worker role as necessary


What You Bring


Must have legal authorization to work in Canada on a full-time basis for anyone other than current employer
Degree in Business, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
Minimum of at least 2 years of experience in data analytics & reporting, business intelligence, or related field working in a complex organization
Proficiency in using analytical applications such as Excel, Power BI, or Tableau in developing solutions is considered an asset
Proficiency in SQL, DAX and advanced data querying with experience extracting from various data platforms
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Ability to collaborate cross-functionally with strong organizational and interpersonal skills, as this role regularly interacts with multiple teams


If you don’t see yourself fully reflected in every job requirement listed in the posting above, we still encourage you to reach out and apply. Research has shown that women & those from under-represented groups often only apply when they feel 100% qualified. We are committed to creating a more equitable, inclusive, and diverse company, and we strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientations, and life experiences to apply.

What We Offer

You bring your skills and experience to Shell and in return you work with talented, committed people in an innovative work environment. You’ll have the opportunity to develop the skills you need to grow in an environment where we value honesty, integrity, and respect for one another. You’ll be able to balance your priorities as you become the best version of yourself.


Progress as a person as we work on innovative projects together
Continuously grow the transferable skills you need to get ahead
Work at the forefront of technology, trends, and practices
Collaborate with experienced colleagues with unique expertise
Achieve your balance in a values-led culture that encourages you to be the best version of yourself
Benefit from flexible working hours, and the possibility of remote/mobile working
Perform at your best with a competitive starting salary and annual performance related salary increase; our pay and benefits packages are among the best in the world
Take advantage of paid parental leave, including for non-birthing parents
Join an organization working to become one of the most diverse and inclusive in the world
We strongly encourage applicants of all genders, ages, ethnicities, cultures, abilities, sexual orientation, and life experiences to apply
Grow as you progress through diverse career opportunities in national and international teams
Gain access to a wide range of training and development programs


We'd like you to know that Shell has a bold goal: to become one of the world’s most diverse and inclusive companies. You can get to know more about how we're working towards that goal, click here.

Shell in Canada

Shell Canada is part of a global group of energy and petrochemicals companies with an average of 92,000 employees in more than 70 countries and has been operating in Canada since 1911.

Shell Canada is one of the few truly integrated energy companies in Canada with all of Shell’s global businesses represented, including upstream, integrated gas, downstream, and renewables and energy solutions. That means we do everything from exploration, gas production, refining and manufacturing, to providing fuels and developing energy solutions for our customers.

At a glance, our businesses and projects in Canada include:


Joint Venture Partner (40%) with LNG Canada which will export Canadian natural gas to Asian markets
Shale gas and liquids assets in Alberta and British Columbia
A network of 1,400 retail stations across Canada
The Scotford Complex in Alberta, includes an upgrader, chemicals plant and is home to the Quest carbon capture and storage facility
The Sarnia Manufacturing Centre refinery in Ontario
Investments in renewables and energy solutions, including EV charging, hydrogen and biofuels
15 Shell-owned distribution terminals, three of which have marine operations
Shell Aviation Canada delivers across a network of 26 airport fixed-based operations and airport consortiums, from coast to coast. Shell Aviation provides 1.7 billion liters of high-quality fuel annually in Canada and offers reliable and safe into-plane delivery
Strong Sulphur and Lubricants businesses as well as an innovative Supply and Trading business

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
City Cast is the leading network of local podcasts and newsletters, with daily shows in cities across the country. We're looking for a Data Analyst to become the connective tissue between our audience, our editorial work, and our business.

As City Cast's Data Analyst, you will be the single source of truth for our audience and revenue data. You'll build and maintain dashboards that bring together listenership, newsletter engagement, web traffic, advertising performance, and subscriber data. You'll forecast where we're headed, surface the patterns behind growth (and decline), and help our staff understand what the numbers are telling us.

This role reports into the Chief Operating Officer and works cross-functionally with editorial, revenue, marketing, and operations.

Deadline to apply: May 28, 2026

What You'll Do


Own City Cast's audience and revenue data end-to-end — from ingestion and cleaning through dashboarding, reporting, and storytelling
Define and steward the metrics that matter to City Cast, working with leadership to align on definitions, benchmarks, and goals.
Work with operations, membership, revenue, finance, editorial teams to build out a robust data ecosystem.
Wrangle and maintain a unified analytics dashboards that pulls from podcast platforms (Megaphone, Apple Podcasts, Spotify), newsletter ESP, web analytics, our CRM, and subscription systems
Design dashboards tailored to different audiences — high-level health metrics for the executive team, market-level performance for editorial, pipeline and pacing for revenue
Forecast audience growth across our network, by city and by line of business, and stress-test those forecasts against real performance. Support finance and sales forecasts
Develop first-party data strategy and maintenance across platforms
Deliver clear, regular insights to staff — what's working, what isn't, what changed this month, and why it matters — through written reports, presentations, and informal coaching
Use AI tools to scale your work — from accelerating dashboard development and data pipeline buildout, to automating routine reporting, to surfacing patterns and anomalies you'd otherwise miss. Help the broader team get more out of our data through AI-assisted workflows
Support specific investigations and decisions — pricing changes, new market launches, format experiments, ad product performance — with rigorous, on-demand analysis


Requirements

What You Bring


You have 4+ years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or audience analytics role, ideally in news, podcasting, or media
You've worked in a media environment and understand the rhythms of a newsroom, the realities of audience growth, and the dynamics of advertising and subscription revenue
You're fluent in SQL and comfortable in at least one BI or visualization tool (Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Mode, Metabase, or similar). Experience with Python or R for analysis is a plus
You're fluent with AI tools and excited to use them in your work — whether that's coding faster with an AI assistant, querying data conversationally, prototyping dashboards, or building lightweight internal tools. You have a clear-eyed sense of where AI accelerates the work and where it needs human judgment
You've built dashboards that people actually use — not just look at once — and you understand the difference
You're a strong communicator who can present findings to non-technical colleagues — including journalists and executives — with clarity and judgment about what matters
You're self-directed and comfortable being the first and only person in this seat. You enjoy building structures where there aren't any yet
You care about local journalism and the communities City Cast serves


Benefits

This position is full-time and remote, with excellent benefits. The salary range is $80,000-$100,000 depending on experience. City Cast is an equal opportunity employer committed to excellence through diversity, which involves attracting talented people from diverse backgrounds and traditions. Read about our mission and core values here. We encourage everyone to apply.

About City Cast: We're a network of one-of-a-kind, daily local news podcasts in cities around the country, accompanied by a daily email newsletter and website that keeps you in the know about what's happening in your community. Currently available in Chicago, DC, Denver, Las Vegas, Madison, Nashville, Philadelphia, Pittsburgh, Portland, Salt Lake, Seattle, and Twin Cities, City Cast is the smartest, easiest way to connect with the place you live and love.

City Cast is an affiliate of Graham Holdings. Read the Graham Holdings Company California Worker Privacy Statement here.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and curious Junior Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will support data collection, analysis, and reporting efforts to help drive informed business decisions. This is an excellent opportunity for an entry-level professional to develop technical and analytical skills in a data-driven environment.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and validate data from multiple sources
Perform basic data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights
Assist in creating dashboards and reports using tools like Excel, Power BI, or Tableau
Write and maintain SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Support senior analysts with ad hoc analysis and reporting requests
Ensure data accuracy and integrity across databases and reports
Document processes, data definitions, and analysis methodologies
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data needs




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
0–2 years of experience in data analysis or a related role (internships count)

Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and curious Junior Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will support data collection, analysis, and reporting efforts to help drive informed business decisions. This is an excellent opportunity for an entry-level professional to develop technical and analytical skills in a data-driven environment.


Key Responsibilities

  • Collect, clean, and validate data from multiple sources
  • Perform basic data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights
  • Assist in creating dashboards and reports using tools like Excel, Power BI, or Tableau
  • Write and maintain SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
  • Support senior analysts with ad hoc analysis and reporting requests
  • Ensure data accuracy and integrity across databases and reports
  • Document processes, data definitions, and analysis methodologies
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data needs


Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
  • 0–2 years of experience in data analysis or a related role (internships count)
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (formulas, pivot tables, charts)
  • Basic knowledge of SQL
  • Familiarity with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Good communication skills to present findings clearly


Preferred Skills

  • Experience with programming languages such as Python or R
  • Understanding of data modeling and database concepts
  • Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipelines
  • Knowledge of basic statistics and data interpretation techniques

Title: Associate Data Analyst
Description:
Collaborating with the Director, Data Design and GWAM Enterprise Data Lake (EDL) Business Support team, the Associate Data Analyst, will be a key member of a team of data professionals responsible for delivering key critical initiatives and establishing the data quality program for Manulife GWAM. You will couple your data analysis experience with a solid understanding of the Investment Segment’s business and operations to implement the data strategy.

Position Responsibilities


You will work with Global Wealth Asset Management Operational Business & Technical Support teams, GWAM Business Teams and the GWAM Chief Data Office to support the Enterprise Data Lake (EDL) daily production activities to ensure data is available, reliable, consistent, accessible, secure, and timely through daily data issue identification, analysis and action planning.
You will partner with the GWAM Chief Data Office Data Governance and Data Analytics, support on-going EDL process improvements, including enhancements to GWAM operational capabilities, GWAM data quality monitoring and controls and GWAM meta data management tools such as the business glossary and data lineage.
Working with different GWAM teams to support EDL design and development for GWAM strategic initiatives.


What We Are Looking For


Highly analytical with strong consulting and problem-solving abilities; tenacity to identify the root cause of problems and apply innovative approaches to solve business data problems
Strong service orientation
Take personal accountability for issue analysis and resolution
Asset management or financial services industry experience
Previous experience in the management of large, firm-wide processes
Effective communication skills
Constructive challenge and influencing skills
Experience in translating between business and technology worlds
Champion of change and effectively managing the implementation of new ideas / improvements
Confident when dealing with investment professionals, senior management and other partners
Knowledge and experience with asset management systems and platforms such as trading/order management, portfolio accounting, client reporting, performance analytics and master data management
Technical Skills, such as knowledge of SQL required to perform data analysis
Familiarity with Microsoft Power Platform tools is considered an asset


Qualifications


1-5 years of relevant experience
SQL (data analysis): 1 year’s hands-on experience
Bachelor’s or Master’s in fields like Finance, Financial Engineering, Accounting, or Business (with concentrations in Investments, Analytics, or Information Systems).
Microsoft Power Platform (Power BI, Power Automate, Power Apps): 1–2 years
Asset management systems/platforms (e.g., OMS/trading, portfolio accounting, client reporting, performance analytics, MDM): 2–5 years
Large, firm‑wide process management: 2–3 years


When You Join Our Team


We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

Title: Data Analyst, Data Intelligence
Description:
Austin, TX or Grand Rapids, MI (Onsite 4 days per week)

Note: This is a full-time role and we do not offer C2C or C2H employment and are not able to sponsor visas for this position.

About Acrisure

Acrisure Enterprise Technology Group is where human expertise meets advanced technology.

A global fintech leader, Acrisure empowers millions of ambitious businesses and individuals with the right solutions to grow boldly forward. Bringing cutting-edge technology and top-tier human support together, we connect clients with customized solutions across a range of insurance, reinsurance, payroll, benefits, cybersecurity, mortgage services – and more.

In the last twelve years, Acrisure has grown in revenue from $38 million to almost $5 billion and employs over 19,000 colleagues in more than 20 countries. Acrisure was built on entrepreneurial spirit. Prioritizing leadership, accountability, and collaboration, we equip our teams to work at the highest levels possible.

The Role

The Data Intelligence team is chartered with advancing data driven decision making across Acrisure. As a member of the Data Intelligence Team, you will be responsible for leading the requirements gathering, design, implementation and delivery of reporting and analytics capabilities for Finance and Accounting Business Functions. You will be comfortable working across a wide variety of people including senior executive leadership, analysts, program management, and technical team members.

Here are some of the ways in which you’ll achieve impact:


Drive the development of efficient and reliable reporting and analytics capabilities with a customer-first mindset.
Advance the transition of users from legacy means of accessing information to the future state strategies of data enablement and self-service.
Create presentation/visualization of data to see beyond the numbers while transforming raw data into actionable insights and recommendations.
Identify, adopt, and evangelize data analysis best practices and technical standards.
Advocate for and identify creative implementations to optimize business impact.
Empower others to grow understanding of the complexity of data by creatively scaling how teams’ knowledge is shared and applied
Help identify better practices, tools, and relevant trends that can improve data quality across the business.


You may be a fit for this role if you have:


5+ years of industry experience as a Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Professional serving the finance and accounting business functions.
A passion for building remarkable things with data.
Demonstrated ability to be a data storyteller and can go deep in building data visualizations and reporting using third party tools (Tableau and PowerBI).
Strong understanding of data models, structures, theories, principles, and practices.
Proficiency working with stakeholders to gather requirements, understand process and translate them to data and create/evaluate associated KPIs.
Experience with SQL, Python, PowerBI, Tableau, dbt, BigQuery, Git, Palantir and data modeling
Experience with agile project execution and business tools such as Jira & ServiceNow
Have excellent communication and leadership skills and can unpack complex problems in order to find solutions.
Ability to understand the business purpose or problem behind a request.
Positive & creative problem solving in the face of data challenges.
Flexibility to successfully multi-task in a fast-paced environment with a positive attitude.
Strong teamwork, solution-centric, and customer-service-oriented attitude.
Possess empathy, kindness towards others, a positive attitude, and self-awareness.
Have a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Math, Statistics, Business, or related field, or equivalent experience.


Candidates should be comfortable with an on-site presence to support collaboration, team leadership, and cross-functional partnership. 

Benefits and Perks:


Competitive compensation
Generous vacation policy, paid holidays, and paid sick time
Medical Insurance, Dental Insurance, and Vision Insurance (employee-paid)
Company-paid Short-Term and Long-Term Disability Insurance
Company-paid Group Life insurance
Company-paid Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Calm App subscription
Employee-paid Pet Insurance and optional supplemental insurance coverage
Vested 401(k) with company match and financial wellness programs
Flexible Spending Account (FSA), Health Savings Account (HSA) and commuter benefits options
Paid maternity leave, paid paternity leave, and fertility benefits
Career growth and learning opportunities
…and so much more!


Please note: This list is not reflective of all benefits. Enrollment waiting periods or eligibility criteria may apply to certain benefits. Offerings may vary based on subsidiary entity or geographic location.

Making a lasting impact on the communities it serves, Acrisure has pledged more than $22 million through its partnerships with Corewell Health Helen DeVos Children's Hospital in Grand Rapids, Michigan, UPMC Children's Hospital in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania and Blythedale Children's Hospital in Valhalla, New York.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates a network of daily local news podcasts, newsletters, and websites across multiple U.S. cities. It addresses the challenge of keeping communities informed by delivering timely local news and information through accessible audio and digital formats. The network spans cities including Chicago, DC, Denver, Las Vegas, Madison, Nashville, Philadelphia, Pittsburgh, Portland, Salt Lake, Seattle, and Twin Cities, offering a comprehensive local news resource.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Data Analys role is critical for integrating audience and revenue data to support strategic decision-making. This position is responsible for consolidating various data sources into actionable insights, forecasting growth, and helping different departments understand performance metrics. The role enables the organization to optimize operations, editorial content, and revenue strategies by providing clear and timely data analysis.




3. Responsibilities:

• Manage audience and revenue data from ingestion through reporting and storytelling.

• Define and maintain key performance metrics in collaboration with leadership.

• Collaborate with operations, membership, revenue, finance, and editorial teams to develop a cohesive data ecosystem.

• Build and maintain unified dashboards integrating data from podcast platforms, newsletters, web analytics, CRM, and subscription systems.

• Create customized dashboards for different stakeholders including executives, editorial, and revenue teams.

• Forecast audience growth by market and business line; validate forecasts against actual performance.

• Develop and maintain first-party data strategies across platforms.

• Provide regular insights and analysis to staff via reports, presentations, and informal coaching.

• Utilize AI tools to enhance dashboard development, reporting automation, and data pattern identification.

• Support targeted analyses for decisions on pricing, new market launches, format experiments, and advertising performance.




4. Requirements:

• Minimum 4 years’ experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or audience analytics, preferably in media.

• Understanding of newsroom dynamics, audience growth, and revenue models including advertising and subscriptions.

• Proficiency in SQL and at least one BI or visualization tool (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI).

• Experience with Python or R is advantageous.

• Skilled in applying AI tools to accelerate and enhance data workflows.

• Proven ability to build effective, user-friendly dashboards.

• Strong communication skills for presenting data insights to non-technical audiences.

• Self-directed and capable of establishing data structures independently.

• Commitment to supporting local journalism and community engagement.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Salary range is $80,000 to $100,000, depending on experience.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• This position is full-time and remote with excellent benefits.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

This position is responsible for consolidating various data sources into actionable insights, forecasting growth, and helping different departments understand performance metrics. The role enables the organization to optimize operations, editorial content, and revenue strategies by providing clear and timely data analysis.


3. Responsibilities:

• Manage audience and revenue data from ingestion through reporting and storytelling.

• Define and maintain key performance metrics in collaboration with leadership.

• Collaborate with operations, membership, revenue, finance, and editorial teams to develop a cohesive data ecosystem.

• Build and maintain unified dashboards integrating data from podcast platforms, newsletters, web analytics, CRM, and subscription systems.

• Create customized dashboards for different stakeholders including executives, editorial, and revenue teams.

• Forecast audience growth by market and business line; validate forecasts against actual performance.

• Develop and maintain first-party data strategies across platforms.

• Provide regular insights and analysis to staff via reports, presentations, and informal coaching.

• Utilize AI tools to enhance dashboard development, reporting automation, and data pattern identification.

• Support targeted analyses for decisions on pricing, new market launches, format experiments, and advertising performance.


4. Requirements:

• Minimum 4 years’ experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or audience analytics, preferably in media.

• Understanding of newsroom dynamics, audience growth, and revenue models including advertising and subscriptions.

• Proficiency in SQL and at least one BI or visualization tool (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI).

• Experience with Python or R is advantageous.

• Skilled in applying AI tools to accelerate and enhance data workflows.

• Proven ability to build effective, user-friendly dashboards.

• Strong communication skills for presenting data insights to non-technical audiences.

• Self-directed and capable of establishing data structures independently.

• Commitment to supporting local journalism and community engagement.


5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Salary range is $80,000 to $100,000, depending on experience.


6. Benefits & Perks:

• This position is full-time and remote with excellent benefits.

Description:
This role is part of the Jobright Direct Hiring Network, where top companies like Cresta AI, Plaud, Mercor, OpenArt, and 1,500+ others hire top talent directly through our platform.




Hiring Company: Matricstek Inc.

Why Join Us:




Role Responsibilities




• Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights

• Experience working with large datasets and relational databases




Preferred

• Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP

• Knowledge of ETL processes and data pipeline development

• Experience with big data tools (Spark, Hadoop)

• Exposure to A/B testing and experimentation

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
McKesson is an impact-driven, Fortune 10 company that touches virtually every aspect of healthcare. We are known for delivering insights, products, and services that make quality care more accessible and affordable. Here, we focus on the health, happiness, and well-being of you and those we serve - we care.










What you do at McKesson matters. We foster a culture where you can grow, make an impact, and are empowered to bring new ideas. Together, we thrive as we shape the future of health for patients, our communities, and our people. If you want to be part of tomorrow's health today, we want to hear from you.
















The Senior Manager, Data & Analytics Platform is responsible for leading a team that provides operational support, administration, and engineering services for enterprise data and analytics platforms. This role focuses on ensuring the stability, performance, and effective use of shared analytics tools and environments that enable data engineering and analytics delivery teams.




This position is accountable for day-to-day platform operations, service reliability, and team execution, partnering closely with Analytical Modernization and other Data Technology teams to support delivery outcomes.










* Our preferred candidate would reside in the Columbus, OH area to support a hybrid work schedule, but we may consider a well-qualified full-remote candidate.




*This position requires current, unrestricted authorization to work in the United States on a permanent basis. We are unable to support or consider candidates on temporary work authorization, including but not limited to F-1 OPT, STEM OPT, CPT, or any status that requires employer sponsorship now or in the future.










Key Responsibilities:




Lead and manage a team of engineers responsible for administration, support, and operational management of data and analytics platforms (e.g., Databricks, Tableau, Power BI)




Ensure platform availability, stability, and performance through defined operational processes, monitoring, and incident management




Oversee user access, environment management, and security integration across data and analytics tools in alignment with enterprise standards




Manage platform-related service requests, support workflows, and prioritization in partnership with delivery teams




Support implementation and rollout of platform capabilities required by data engineering and analytics teams




Partner with architecture, security, and enterprise technology teams to ensure platform configurations align with standards and requirements




Monitor and manage cloud platform usage and costs related to analytics tooling, identifying opportunities for optimization




Coordinate platform upgrades, patching, and release activities in collaboration with external teams and vendors




Provide input into platform roadmap and improvements based on operational insights and team experience




Establish and reinforce standard operating procedures, documentation, and support models for platform services










People Leadership:




Directly manage and develop a team of engineers (typically 6-8 direct reports)




Set clear expectations for performance, delivery, and operational excellence




Provide coaching, feedback, and development opportunities to grow team capability




Ensure consistent execution of team processes, priorities, and service commitments










Required Experience & Skills:




Typically 9+ years of professional experience and 2+ years of management experience




Hands-on experience supporting and administering data and analytics platforms, with specific experience in Databricks, BI tools, and cloud-based data environments




Strong understanding of operational support models, including incident management, service management, and platform reliability




Experience working in cloud environments (Azure preferred) with awareness of cost and performance considerations




Ability to manage competing priorities and support requests in a fast-paced, delivery-driven environment




Strong collaboration skills with the ability to work across engineering, architecture, and business teams




Clear communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts for different audiences










Preferred Experience:




Experience supporting modern data platforms and analytics tooling in a shared services model




Familiarity with data engineering workflows and how platform services enable delivery teams




Experience working in environments with both modern and legacy technologies










We are proud to offer a competitive compensation package at McKesson as part of our Total Rewards. This is determined by several factors, including performance, experience and skills, equity, regular job market evaluations, and geographical markets. The pay range shown below is aligned with McKesson's pay philosophy, and pay will always be compliant with any applicable regulations. In addition to base pay, other compensation, such as an annual bonus or long-term incentive opportunities may be offered. For more information regarding benefits at McKesson, pleaseclick here.










Our Base Pay Range for this position










$131,600 - $219,400

In light of these scams, please bear the following in mind:




McKesson Talent Advisors will never solicit money or credit card information in connection with a McKesson job application.







McKesson Talent Advisors do not communicate with candidates via online chatrooms or using email accounts such as Gmail or Hotmail. Note that McKesson does rely on a virtual assistant (Gia) for certain recruiting-related communications with candidates.










McKesson job postings are posted on our career site: careers.mckesson.com.

For additional information on McKesson's full Equal Employment Opportunity policies, visit our Equal Employment Opportunity page.

Title: Market Insights Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Business Analyst (Analyst, Competitive Strategy & Insights)

Location: Remote

Duration: 6 Months with possible extension

Max Pay rate: $49/hr




Key Responsibilities

Lead international market sizing analyses across priority regions using multiple data sources, including internal data, third-party research, web data, and industry benchmarks.
Build robust bottom-up and top-down models in Excel to estimate Total Addressable Market (TAM), category penetration, growth opportunities, and market trends.
Analyze financial statements, annual reports, and industry publications to identify key trends and develop business models.
Evaluate competitor performance and positioning across regions, including traffic trends, GMV proxies, pricing strategies, supply dynamics, and assortment.
Design and maintain standardized Excel frameworks to track market size, growth rates, and competitor performance metrics over time.
Create clean, scalable datasets by aggregating, validating, and normalizing data from multiple internal and external sources.
Conduct quantitative benchmarking across competitors, markets, and categories to assess relative market position and identify opportunities.
Perform in-depth desktop research and synthesize findings into quantitative models and qualitative insights that inform business strategy and performance.
Manage external vendor relationships to ensure high-quality data delivery, while proactively identifying gaps and driving continuous improvements.
Partner with leadership and cross-functional teams to provide data-driven recommendations for strategic planning and decision-making.
Manage multiple concurrent projects with clear plans, stakeholder alignment, and timely delivery of key milestones and deliverables.

What You Need to Succeed

Bachelor’s degree (BA/BS) with 5+ years of experience in strategy, analytics, consulting, or e-commerce insights.
Strong business acumen with a solid understanding of e-commerce or marketplace models, including buyer/seller dynamics, pricing, assortment, and conversion drivers.
Advanced Excel skills with strong modeling and analytical capabilities.
Excellent structured problem-solving and quantitative reasoning skills.
Proven ability to convert complex data into clear insights and compelling executive-level presentations.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence mid-level and senior leaders.
Experience working cross-functionally in fast-paced, dynamic, and ambiguous environments.
Strong project management and organizational skills, with the ability to prioritize and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.
High ownership mindset, intellectual curiosity, and a continuous improvement approach.
Collaborative, positive, and easy-to-work-with attitude.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in e-commerce or marketplace platforms.
Proficiency in SQL for data extraction, querying, and analysis.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Looker.
Experience working with large, complex datasets.
MBA or equivalent advanced degree.

Title: Database Administrator
Description:
We connect top talent in Business Services, Tech, Marketing & Creative with companies of all sizes—offering flexible, high-touch staffing solutions. We are currently filling the following contract job for our client.

Job Title: Database Administrator 

Overview

Join a pioneering company in the identity intelligence space as a Database Administrator, where your expertise will directly impact the security, efficiency, and reliability of mission-critical data platforms. In this role, you will be at the forefront of managing and optimizing complex databases that power innovative solutions used globally for risk mitigation, fraud prevention, and regulatory compliance. If you thrive in a dynamic environment that values problem-solving, performance tuning, and scalable architecture, this is your chance to make a meaningful difference.

Required Skills

Proven experience with AWS RDS environments, emphasizing performance, security, and availability
Strong proficiency with SQL, especially PostgreSQL and MySQL
Hands-on experience with NoSQL databases such as MongoDB and DynamoDB
Expertise in upgrading databases with minimal downtime
Ability to scale databases both horizontally and vertically
Intermediate proficiency in Python for automation and scripting
Experience with database monitoring frameworks and automation tools
Knowledge of AWS ETL platforms, particularly AWS DMS
Solid understanding of Linux fundamentals, including networking, security, and file systems
Demonstrated ability to design, deploy, and administer complex AWS database solutions
Relevant certifications such as AWS Solutions Architect or Developer are preferred

Nice to Have Skills

Experience with self-healing automation, system health monitoring, and diagnostics
Familiarity with performance tuning and troubleshooting at the database level
Prior experience working in fast-paced, cloud-first environments
Knowledge of additional cloud services and infrastructure tools

Preferred Education and Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field
At least 3-5 years of relevant DBA experience, with a focus on cloud-based database environments and SaaS platforms

MissionStaff is an equal opportunity employer. Please note that we do not accept unsolicited resumes from third-party recruiters or agencies. Additionally, candidates must be U.S. citizens or Permanent Residents to be considered for this position.
