Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Company Overview

Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the #1 company in e-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM).

What you'll do

As a Senior Data Analyst, you are deeply curious about data and passionate about storytelling, with a strong ability to identify trends and patterns using analytics tools and methodologies. You are comfortable presenting analytics-driven insights to diverse audiences and tailoring messages based on technical aptitude. In this role, you will support top-line growth and retention metrics across Docusign by improving data quality, driving internal adoption, and delivering high-impact analysis for Field Sales. You will build clear, compelling data narratives that help executives understand opportunities and accelerate decision-making, while collaborating closely with our Field Teams, including Sales and Customer Success to translate data needs into actionable insights.

This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Senior Manager, Product Management.

Responsibility


Act as a data champion, fostering a culture of curiosity and continuous improvement by identifying new opportunities to leverage data for business growth and operational excellence
Provide data-driven support for escalations and enhancement requests by quantifying impact and prioritizing interventions
Leverage advanced analytics and hands-on experience with AI tools to streamline data analysis, automate reporting, and accelerate business decision-making for sales and executive stakeholders
Explore large-scale datasets using SQL and AI-assisted analytics platforms to uncover actionable insights, identify trends, and solve complex business problems
Partner with Product managers to build predictive models, KPIs and AI-driven insights to drive improved analytics and data democratization across Docusign
Develop executive-ready dashboards and visualizations to communicate customer health & usage, and strategic opportunities for our Account Teams to drive growth
Drive AI-enabled automation, self-service analytics, and data governance to improve efficiency, scalability, and insight quality


Job Designation

Hybrid: Employee divides their time between in-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: Minimum 2 days per week; may vary by team but will be weekly in-office expectation)

Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law.

What you bring

Basic


Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related discipline
8+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, preferably in customer success, GTM, or sales role supporting internal teams
Proven expertise navigating Tableau, Salesforce and Snowflake
Experience using AI models and tools to streamline data analytics and generate insights
Strong proficiency in SQL and experience working with large data sets
Solid functional understanding of sales lifecycle with background in SaaS
Strong analytical mindset and a passion for extracting meaningful insights
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to present complex findings in a clear and concise manner


Preferred


Knowledge in subscription business model
Excellent written and verbal communication skills


Life at Docusign

Working here

Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal.

We each have a responsibility to ensure every team member has an equal opportunity to succeed, to be heard, to exchange ideas openly, to build lasting relationships, and to do the work of their life. Best of all, you will be able to feel deep pride in the work you do, because your contribution helps us make the world better than we found it. And for that, you’ll be loved by us, our customers, and the world in which we live.

Accommodation

Docusign is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need such an accommodation, or a religious accommodation, during the application process, please contact us at accommodations@docusign.com.

If you experience any issues, concerns, or technical difficulties during the application process please get in touch with our Talent organization at taops@docusign.com for assistance.

Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice


Show more
HTML:
Company Overview

Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the #1 company in e-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM).

What you'll do

As a Senior Data Analyst, you are deeply curious about data and passionate about storytelling, with a strong ability to identify trends and patterns using analytics tools and methodologies. You are comfortable presenting analytics-driven insights to diverse audiences and tailoring messages based on technical aptitude. In this role, you will support top-line growth and retention metrics across Docusign by improving data quality, driving internal adoption, and delivering high-impact analysis for Field Sales. You will build clear, compelling data narratives that help executives understand opportunities and accelerate decision-making, while collaborating closely with our Field Teams, including Sales and Customer Success to translate data needs into actionable insights.

This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Senior Manager, Product Management.

Responsibility

  • Act as a data champion, fostering a culture of curiosity and continuous improvement by identifying new opportunities to leverage data for business growth and operational excellence
  • Provide data-driven support for escalations and enhancement requests by quantifying impact and prioritizing interventions
  • Leverage advanced analytics and hands-on experience with AI tools to streamline data analysis, automate reporting, and accelerate business decision-making for sales and executive stakeholders
  • Explore large-scale datasets using SQL and AI-assisted analytics platforms to uncover actionable insights, identify trends, and solve complex business problems
  • Partner with Product managers to build predictive models, KPIs and AI-driven insights to drive improved analytics and data democratization across Docusign
  • Develop executive-ready dashboards and visualizations to communicate customer health & usage, and strategic opportunities for our Account Teams to drive growth
  • Drive AI-enabled automation, self-service analytics, and data governance to improve efficiency, scalability, and insight quality

Job Designation

Hybrid: Employee divides their time between in-office and remote work. Access to an office location is required. (Frequency: Minimum 2 days per week; may vary by team but will be weekly in-office expectation)

Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law.

What you bring

Basic

  • Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related discipline
  • 8+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, preferably in customer success, GTM, or sales role supporting internal teams
  • Proven expertise navigating Tableau, Salesforce and Snowflake
  • Experience using AI models and tools to streamline data analytics and generate insights
  • Strong proficiency in SQL and experience working with large data sets
  • Solid functional understanding of sales lifecycle with background in SaaS
  • Strong analytical mindset and a passion for extracting meaningful insights
  • Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to present complex findings in a clear and concise manner

Preferred

  • Knowledge in subscription business model
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Life at Docusign

Working here

Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal.

Title: DATA ANALYST
HTML:
CRIF, azienda globale leader nei sistemi di informazioni creditizie, business information, analytics, outsourcing e soluzioni digitali avanzate per lo sviluppo del business e l’open banking, ricerca un


Data analyst


La risorsa si occuperà di collaborare alle attività di Data Management e Data Elaboration nell’ambito del servizio Legal Eye, per Cribis credit management, società del gruppo.


Le attività :


  • elaborazione di informazioni strutturate e non strutturate, combinando metadati automatici e strumenti di Intelligenza Artificiale, al fine di fornire: servizi di arricchimento informativo; dashboard di monitoraggio e benchmarking; supporto decisionale tramite modelli di predictive justice.
  • Partecipazione al piano di evoluzione e digitalizzazione della Practice, contribuendo al test e allo sviluppo di nuove soluzioni basate su Intelligenza Artificiale, finalizzate all’automazione e all’efficientamento dei processi di credit management.
  • Collaborare in modo continuativo con: l’area Operations, per la raccolta e la comprensione dei requisiti di business e per le attività di testing delle soluzioni; l’area IT, per lo sviluppo e l’implementazione delle soluzioni nel rispetto delle linee guida aziendali.
  • Spiccate capacità di teamwork e di collaborazione interfunzionale;
  • Attitudine alla comprensione di processi di business;
  • Capacità analitica unita a un approccio proattivo e orientato alla ricerca di soluzioni innovative.


REQUISTI

  • Familiarità con strumenti emergenti di Intelligenza Artificiale (es. LLM, strumenti di AI-assisted analytics) e interesse ad approfondirne l’utilizzo in contesti di automazione e supporto decisionale
  • Laurea in discipline informatiche, ingegneristiche o statistiche, oppure esperienza professionale equivalente;
  • Buona conoscenza di MS Office, SQL e API REST o altri linguaggi di programmazione;
  • Costituisce titolo preferenziale la conoscenza del linguaggio Python;
  • Almeno un anno di esperienza lavorativa, maturata in CRIF o in contesti analoghi.
  • Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese sia scritta sia parlata;
  • Conoscenza madrelingua della lingua italiana.


sede di lavoro: Bologna

Title: Finance business data analyst H/F
Description:
Le poste




Votre mission

En tant que Business Intelligence analyst vous jouez un rôle clé dans la transformation des données en levier de performance. Au cœur des enjeux business vous serez responsable de la collecte, l'analyse et la valorisation des données afin de soutenir la prise de décision stratégique et opérationnelle.

Le BA agit comme un pont entre les équipes techniques et les équipes métiers transformant les données complexes en Insight clair exploitable et orienté business.




Mission principale




Gestion et qualité des données

Identifier participer à l'intégration de nouvelles sources de données
Collaborer avec les équipes IT/data pour garantir la qualité et l'intégrité des données glas
Maintenir les tables et le référentiel des spécificités des applications

Analyse et exploitation des données

Collecter nettoyer et structurer les données issues des sources multiples.
Analyser les données pour identifier tendance anomalie et opportunités.
Réaliser des analyses ad hoc en réponse aux besoins métiers.

Data visualisation reporting

Concevoir développer et maintenir les Dashboard interactif et sous Power bi
Produire des reporting réguliers et fiables garantir la fiabilité et la lisibilité et la pertinence des reporting/ indicateurs
Assurer la production régulière de rapports standards suivi de performance à destination des équipes marketing vente et direction

Production Insight

Transformer des données complexes en recommandations actionnables.
Aider les équipes métiers à comprendre et exploiter les résultats







Profil recherché







De formation supérieure en data, informatique ou business, vous possédez une expérience en Business Intelligence ou data analysis de 4 ans. Anglais cpourant afin d egérer de séchanges à l'international.

Compétences techniques

Maîtrise des outils de data visualisation, idéalement Power BI
Bonne connaissance des bases de données et du traitement de données (SQL recommandé)
Capacité à collecter, nettoyer, structurer et fiabiliser des données issues de sources multiples
Compréhension des enjeux de qualité, intégrité et gouvernance des données
Aisance avec les analyses ad hoc et le suivi d’indicateurs de performance (KPI)

Compétences analytiques & business

Excellente capacité d’analyse et de synthèse
Aptitude à identifier tendances, anomalies et opportunités à partir des données
Forte orientation business et prise de décision
Capacité à traduire les besoins métiers en indicateurs pertinents et exploitables







Avantages Mayoly

Package salariale incluant une rémunération variable
Excellente protection sociale complète (80% de cotisation mutuelle prise en charge par l’entreprise, prévoyance)
Offre épargne salariale complète (PEE, PERO CET participation intéressement)
Nombreux avantages CSE
Politique d’inclusion et diversité engagée

Title: Junior Data Analyst
HTML:
We’re Hiring: Junior Data Analyts — Rhino, are you out there?


At WE ARE META, we connect Rhinos to projects where they can truly shine. If you are looking for growth, balance, and a team that truly supports you, this is the place for you. After all, we are experts at transforming raw talent into unstoppable force.

• Cool welcome kit waiting for you 🎒

• Career progression, no corporate maze 📈

• Health insurance to keep you in hero mode 🏥

• Coverflex meal card 🍽️

• Minimum 1 year of experience in a Data Analyst, Reporting Analyst, or similar role;

• Good knowledge of SQL for querying, validating, and analyzing data;

• Basic knowledge of Python for data analysis or automation tasks;

• Experience using Power BI to create and maintain dashboards and reports;

Important:

This position is following a hybrid regime, 2x/week, in Lisbon. Candidates should be based in Portugal and have a Portuguese or other EU citizenship, or a valid Portuguese work permit to be considered.

Title: Business Data Analyst
HTML:
Immobiliare.it S.p.A. è un Gruppo italiano composto da società specializzate in servizi Digital Tech per la compravendita e l’affitto di immobili. Nata nel 2005, l'azienda opera sia sul mercato nazionale che su scala internazionale, rivolgendosi a privati, professionisti del real estate, istituti bancari e operatori finanziari. La presenza del Gruppo si estende inoltre in Spagna (Pisos.com, Indomio.es, Enalquiler.com), Grecia (Spitogatos.gr, Indomio.gr), Slovenia (Nekretnine.net) e Croazia (Crozilla.com, Indomio.hr).


In un’ottica di potenziamento del Team di Business Intelligence, siamo alla ricerca di una figura di Business Data Analyst che avrà la responsabilità di supportare l’organizzazione attraverso l’analisi dei dati, con l'obiettivo di ottimizzare l'efficienza e incrementare il business aziendale.


Le tue principali attività:

· lavorare a stretto contatto con il centro studi interno per monitorare i trend del settore;

· leggere, analizzare e interpretare report e dati relativi al mercato immobiliare proveniente da una pluralità di fonti esterne;

· realizzare analisi strategiche e dettagliate, evidenziando le problematiche, le opportunità e le soluzioni per il business tramite l’interpretazione dei dati;

· identificare insight rilevanti e renderli fruibili alle diverse Business Unit;

· ottimizzare dei modelli di analisi esistenti e predisporre nuovi strumenti di monitoraggio;

· creare report ed analisi di flussi dati.


Requisiti:

· laurea in discipline economiche, matematiche o ingegneristiche;

· esperienza di almeno 1/2 anni nel ruolo;

· capacità di presentare analisi e dati in modo chiaro e strutturato;

· ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Office, nello specifico Excel;

· ottima conoscenza di Tableau.


Requisiti preferenziali:

· conoscenza di SQL e/o linguaggi di programmazione come Python, R o JavaScript;

· esperienza con piattaforme di Business Intelligence.


Luogo di lavoro:

Milano (zona Stazione Garibaldi)


Orario di lavoro:

Full time


Tipo e durata del contratto e Retribuzione:

Commisurati all’esperienza pregressa



Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
We are HCLTech, one of the fastest-growing large tech companies in the world and home to 225,000+ people across 60 countries, supercharging progress through industry-leading capabilities centered around Digital, Engineering and Cloud. The driving force behind that work, our people, are diverse, creative, and passionate, raising the bar for excellence on a regular basis.




Role Overview

We are looking for Junior Data Analysts to support analysis and reporting activities by preparing, exploring, and interpreting data to help the business make informed decisions.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and validate data from various sources.
Analyze datasets to identify patterns, trends, and insights.
Support the development of reports and dashboards.
Assist business stakeholders with data interpretation.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and quality.




Required Skills

Strong knowledge of:

SQL.
Microsoft Excel (advanced level).

Familiarity with:

Data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.).

Basic understanding of:

Data analysis and statistics.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.




Candidate Profile

Education

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in IT / Computer Science / related field.
Graduated from a university in Romania or Europe.




Experience

0–3 years of experience.
Fresh graduates with strong fundamentals are also considered.




General Requirements

Strong communication and collaboration skills.
High attention to detail.
Eagerness to learn and grow professionally.
Solid foundation in data-related concepts.




Selection Criteria & Process

Available for immediate start or short notice.
Open to on-site / hybrid work (remote excluded).

Willing to attend:

2 interview rounds (HCLTech TP1 + Customer interview).




What do we offer?

25 holiday days per year.
1066 RON monthly budget in Benefit Online Platform.
Life Insurance cover.
The employees working on bank holidays will be benefitting either of a paid day off in the next 30 days + 100% of the hourly base salary for that specific day OR 200% of the hourly base salary for that specific day.
Reimbursement for eyeglasses – max 1500 RON to be claimed every 3 years.
Childbirth allowance of 7000 RON after 1 year in HCLTech.
Internal trainings and certifications.




*HCLTech is committed to protecting and securing the privacy and confidentiality of the Personal Data which it collects directly or indirectly from you when applying for a job at HCLTech either directly or through a third-party human resources agency. This notice (the “Notice”) outlines and explains how HCL Technologies Limited including its subsidiaries, local employing entities, associates, and affiliated companies [collectively referred to as “HCLTech”, “us,” “our”, or “we”] will process your Personal Data in accordance with applicable privacy legislation(s).

Title: Business & Data Analyst
Place: Sant Cugat del Vallès, Catalonia, Spain
Description:
¿Qué estamos buscando?




Actualmente nos encontramos en búsqueda de nuestro/nuestra futuro/futura Business Consultant & Data Analyst cuya misión es diseñar, analizar, controlar y optimizar los procesos operativos de las áreas bajo su responsabilidad dentro de la Dirección de Operaciones, aportando inteligencia de negocio y visión analítica para garantizar la eficiencia, la calidad del servicio y la escalabilidad de los procesos.




La persona seleccionada será responsable del análisis de datos operativos, el seguimiento de KPIs, cuadros de mando y SLA’s, con el objetivo de detectar desviaciones, anticipar tendencias y proponer mejoras continuas que contribuyan al cumplimiento de los objetivos operativos y presupuestarios de la compañía.




Asimismo, velará para que los procesos operativos sean robustos, eficientes, alineados con la normativa vigente y con los estándares de calidad establecidos, asegurando una correcta ejecución tanto por equipos internos como externos.







Hablemos del Proyecto...




Como Business Consultant & Data Analyst tu misión será:




Analizar la viabilidad de nuevas tareas o procesos dentro de la Unidad de Operaciones, elaborando informes en los que se recojan las necesidades, el impacto y el coste/esfuerzo asociado.
Analizar el comportamiento de los distintos servicios mediante datos, cuadros de mando e indicadores operativos para identificar oportunidades de mejora y anticipar posibles desviaciones.
Diseñar y documentar los procesos operativos, definiendo los flujos detallados y asegurando el cumplimiento normativo con el menor coste posible para la compañía.
Asegurar la correcta ejecución de los procesos por parte de personal interno y/o externo, garantizando la alineación con el modelo operativo definido.
Realizar el seguimiento de los KPIs operativos y promover acciones correctoras en caso de detectar desviaciones o ineficiencias.
Supervisar las auditorías de calidad y el cumplimiento de los niveles de servicio y estándares establecidos.
Mantener una interlocución continua con las áreas de negocio para la revisión de necesidades, definición de planes de acción y resolución de incidencias operativas.







¿Qué valoramos de tu candidatura?




Posees Licenciatura o Grado en Administración y Dirección de Empresas, Economía, Ingeniería, Matemáticas, Estadística o similar. Se valorará formación complementaria en análisis de datos, gestión de procesos, mejora continua (Lean, Six Sigma) u otras metodologías afines.
Tienes experiencia mínima de 3 años en posiciones similares en áreas de Operaciones, Procesos, Control de Gestión o Analítica. Experiencia demostrable en diseño, definición y optimización de procesos operativos.
Posees experiencia en el seguimiento de KPIs, elaboración de cuadros de mando y control de SLA’s. Valorable experiencia en entornos financieros, financieros no bancarios o de servicios.







¿Qué te ofrecemos?




Serás parte de un equipo de profesionales de primer nivel, con una excelente reputación en el sector.
Podrás disfrutar de una amplia oferta formativa.
Aprenderás de la mano de Managers de referencia de las distintas direcciones.
Ampliarás tu red de contactos a través de la colaboración y espacios de networking.
Contarás con opciones de desarrollo de carrera y movilidad dentro del Grupo. Somos un grupo bancario que sienta sus bases en los principios de meritocracia y el desarrollo de sus colaboradores.
Contratación indefinida y remuneración competitiva.







Banco Sabadell está comprometido en promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, procurando el desarrollo profesional de la plantilla y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, orientación sexual, religión, etnia o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social.




Banco Sabadell forma parte de la red de empresas con el distintivo de “Igualdad en la Empresa” otorgado por el Ministerio de Igualdad.

  • Realizar el seguimiento de los KPIs operativos y promover acciones correctoras en caso de detectar desviaciones o ineficiencias.
  • Supervisar las auditorías de calidad y el cumplimiento de los niveles de servicio y estándares establecidos.
  • Mantener una interlocución continua con las áreas de negocio para la revisión de necesidades, definición de planes de acción y resolución de incidencias operativas.



¿Qué valoramos de tu candidatura?


  • Posees Licenciatura o Grado en Administración y Dirección de Empresas, Economía, Ingeniería, Matemáticas, Estadística o similar. Se valorará formación complementaria en análisis de datos, gestión de procesos, mejora continua (Lean, Six Sigma) u otras metodologías afines.
  • Tienes experiencia mínima de 3 años en posiciones similares en áreas de Operaciones, Procesos, Control de Gestión o Analítica. Experiencia demostrable en diseño, definición y optimización de procesos operativos.
  • Posees experiencia en el seguimiento de KPIs, elaboración de cuadros de mando y control de SLA’s. Valorable experiencia en entornos financieros, financieros no bancarios o de servicios.



¿Qué te ofrecemos?


  • Serás parte de un equipo de profesionales de primer nivel, con una excelente reputación en el sector.
  • Podrás disfrutar de una amplia oferta formativa.
  • Aprenderás de la mano de Managers de referencia de las distintas direcciones.
  • Ampliarás tu red de contactos a través de la colaboración y espacios de networking.
  • Contarás con opciones de desarrollo de carrera y movilidad dentro del Grupo. Somos un grupo bancario que sienta sus bases en los principios de meritocracia y el desarrollo de sus colaboradores.
  • Contratación indefinida y remuneración competitiva.



Banco Sabadell está comprometido en promover ambientes de trabajo en los que se trate con respeto y dignidad a las personas, procurando el desarrollo profesional de la plantilla y garantizando la igualdad de oportunidades en su selección, formación y promoción ofreciendo un entorno de trabajo libre de cualquier discriminación por motivo de género, edad, orientación sexual, religión, etnia o cualquier otra circunstancia personal o social.


Banco Sabadell forma parte de la red de empresas con el distintivo de “Igualdad en la Empresa” otorgado por el Ministerio de Igualdad.

Title: Data Analyst / Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst / Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst (m/w/d) – Schwerpunkt Reporting & Analytics




Die Auktion & Markt AG zählt mit der Marke Autobid.de zu den führenden Auktionshäusern im Bereich Kfz-Remarketing in Europa. Der Geschäftsbereich führt Gebrauchtwagen-Auktionen exklusiv für Kfz-Händler durch. Das Familienunternehmen mit 35 Jahren Erfahrung beschäftigt deutschlandweit aktuell über 400 Mitarbeiter.




Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams ist in der Unternehmenszentrale in Wiesbaden ab sofort folgende Stelle in Vollzeit zu besetzen:




Data Analyst / Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst (m/w/d) – Schwerpunkt Reporting & Analytics




Als aktiver Sparringspartner bist du verantwortlich dafür, dass aus Daten klare und belastbare Entscheidungsgrundlagen entstehen. Du generierst echte Mehrwerte aus Zahlen und sorgst dafür, dass unsere Fachbereiche, das Management und die Produktteams jederzeit verstehen, was wirklich passiert – und warum. Dabei arbeitest du nah an den Entscheidern und beeinflusst direkt die Steuerung des Unternehmens




Deine Aufgaben

Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung von Reporting- und Analyse-Lösungen (insb. IBM Cognos)
Konzeption und Umsetzung von Dashboards, Reports und Kennzahlenmodellen
Analyse von Geschäftsprozessen und Ableitung relevanter KPIs
Sicherstellung von Datenqualität, Konsistenz und Nachvollziehbarkeit
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen, Produktteams und IT
Unterstützung bei der datenbasierten Entscheidungsfindung
Identifikation von Potenzialen zur Automatisierung und Optimierung durch Daten




Dein Profil

Erfahrung im Bereich Business Intelligence / Data Analytics
Fundierte Kenntnisse in IBM Cognos (Reporting, Modellierung, Framework Manager)
Verständnis für Datenstrukturen, Datenmodelle und Schnittstellen
Fähigkeit, komplexe Daten verständlich aufzubereiten
Analytisches Denkvermögen und strukturierte Arbeitsweise
Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift. Kenntnisse der polnischen Sprache (Kooperation mit polnischem Dienstleister) sind von Vorteil, stellen aber keine Bedingung dar




Wir bieten

Unbefristeter Arbeitsvertrag
30 Tage Urlaub
Zusätzliche arbeitsfreie Tage (24.12. und 31.12.)
Jobrad
Regelmäßige Mitarbeiterfeste
Betriebliche Altersvorsorge / Vermögenswirksame Leistungen
Vergünstigte Event-Tickets
Arbeitsmedizinische Betreuung

Show more

Title: Junior - Data Analyst - Milano
Description:
Junior - Data & Automation Analyst - Milano

Sei pronto a #MakeAnImpactThatMatters nel nostro team Audit & Assurance?

Entrando nel Team Data Analytics Audit & Assurance avrai l’occasione di crescere e sviluppare forti competenze trasversali, lavorando su progetti sfidanti e collaborando con moltissimi clienti. Che tu stia analizzando dati complessi o progettando soluzioni di automazione, sarai sempre al centro di iniziative che trasformano i processi aziendali.

Con la supervisione dei colleghi e colleghe più esperti, sarai responsabile delle attività progettuali nelle quali sarai coinvolto, e della qualità del tuo lavoro e del tuo allineamento alle priorità, agli obiettivi e alle esigenze dei clienti.

Grazie a questa esperienza, potrai acquisire un bagaglio culturale utile e ampio in questo ambito operando in una realtà leader nel settore.

#YourRole

In quanto Analyst avrai la possibilità di imparare dai colleghi più Senior (attraverso il learning on the job) e sviluppare competenze tecniche e relazionali lavorando in un contesto stimolante, che comporta sfide impegnative con ottime prospettive professionali.

La tua missione sarà supportare la trasformazione digitale e l’efficientamento dei processi aziendali attraverso l’analisi dei dati e l’implementazione di soluzioni tecnologiche innovative come l’intelligenza artificiale. Sarai parte di un team che sta ridefinendo come l’AI può creare valore aggiunto nei processi, anche in quelli audit e compliance.

In Particolare, Ti Occuperai Di


Raccogliere, analizzare e interpretare dati provenienti da diverse fonti mediante strumenti quali Excel, SQL, Python, per identificare opportunità di ottimizzazione e automazione dei processi
Sviluppare report e dashboard tramite i principali tool di BI (PowerBI, Qlik Sense, Qlik Cloud) per fornire insights chiari ai clienti
Supportare nella pulizia, organizzazione e gestione dei dataset su database relazionali e NoSQL, garantendo la qualità dei dati per l’analisi
Analizzare e ridisegnare processi aziendali (in particolare nell’area AFC - Accounting, Finance & Compliance) identificando inefficienze e opportunità di automazione
Implementare soluzioni di hyperautomation e AI combinando diverse tecnologie e approcci innovativi per automatizzare processi complessi, integrando funzionalità native con sviluppi custom mediante linguaggi di programmazione (C#, Python)
Fornire insights strategici per il miglioramento e l’automazione dei processi aziendali, trasformando i dati in opportunità concrete di efficientamento


Grazie all’ufficio Learning avrai modo di seguire corsi e attività di formazione che ti permetteranno di ampliare e approfondire le tue conoscenze.

I colleghi del Talent invece, si occuperanno in maniera strutturata del tuo avanzamento di carriera con KPI e obiettivi definiti, che se raggiunti, ti garantiranno la revisione del grade e degli economics.

#WinningRequirements

Requisiti Fondamentali


Laurea in Ingegneria Informatica, Informatica, Data Science o materie affini
Conoscenza di SQL e database relazionali (MySQL, SQL Server)
Conoscenza di linguaggi di programmazione per l’analisi e l’automazione dei dati (Python, C#, R)
Conoscenza base di ML/AI (supervised/unsupervised learning, valutazione dei modelli)
Conoscenza dei concetti di organizzazione di un Data Warehouse
Spiccate capacità analitiche e attitudine al problem-solving
Propensione al lavoro in team
Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese
Disponibilità a viaggiare in Italia e all’estero


#NiceToHave


Esperienza su strumenti quali SQL Server, Power BI, Qlik, PowerPlatform
Conoscenza di Python per automazione e scripting
Conoscenza delle principali tecniche di machine learning, deep learning e NLP
Esperienza nell’utilizzo di framework di ML/AI (TensorFlow, PyTorch,...)
Confidenza con strumenti di Process Mining (Celonis)
Esperienza con strumenti RPA (UiPath, Blue Prism, Power Automate)
Nozioni di data governance
Conoscenza base di EU AI Act e framework normativi di riferimento (es. ISO 42001)


#LoveWorkingAtDeloitteIf


Vorresti intraprendere una carriera professionale nel mondo della consulenza, dove la parola d’ordine è: crescita
L’idea di trasformare un problema in opportunità ti stimola
Ti ritieni una persona empatica e quindi disponibile ad accogliere le diverse esigenze dei tuoi colleghi e degli stakeholder interni ed esterni
Ti piacerebbe imparare ad avere una visione progettuale d’insieme, tenendo sempre a mente l’operatività per raggiungere gli obiettivi con integrità e rispetto degli standard Deloitte
Ti piace lavorare in un ambiente sfidante in cui le priorità possono cambiare quotidianamente
Ami lavorare in team
Sei affascinato dalla possibilità di combinare analisi dei dati, intelligenza artificiale e automazione per risolvere problemi complessi
Sei entusiasta di contribuire all’evoluzione dell’Assurance AI e delle soluzioni intelligenti nel settore dell’audit e compliance


#MakeanImpactThatMatters

Siamo impegnati a ridurre i nostri impatti e incoraggiamo le persone ad assumere comportamenti sostenibili. Per salvaguardare l’ambiente e limitare il climate change, abbiamo sviluppato la strategia WorldClimate, attraverso la quale ci siamo posti l’obiettivo di diventare una Net Zero.

Join our #iAct movement! Fai il quiz per calcolare la tua carbon footprint e scopri come adottare dei comportamenti più responsabili.

Dettagli Offerta


Inserimento: Stage di 6 mesi caratterizzato da un percorso di training ad alto valore formativo
Sede di lavoro: Milano


Inclusione & Diversità

Saranno prese in considerazione tutte le candidature indistintamente, nel rispetto dei nostri valori ed in linea con la nostra cultura inclusiva.

Se vuoi scoprire come sarà il processo di selezione, guarda questo video.

Se vuoi prepararti ad incontrare i nostri Recruiter iscriviti alla nostra piattaforma gratuita di orientamento al lavoro.

Title: Data Analyst (Product)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists ‌work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a talented Data Analyst to dig into our complex databases, look for root causes of a problem, design your own solutions, and write your own code to implement them. We aim for you to never stop learning, picking up new skills and delivering value along the way.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through our data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and service teams
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Maintaining and creating new dashboards to track metrics and visualise insights
Promoting data literacy across the company, organising and holding workshops


What You'll Need


Previous experience in an analytical role, creating impactful solutions
A background/education in a quantitative discipline
5+ years of experience with SQL
Great skills with Python or other programming languages
Evidence of solid mathematical and statistics knowledge


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists ‌work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a talented Data Analyst to dig into our complex databases, look for root causes of a problem, design your own solutions, and write your own code to implement them. We aim for you to never stop learning, picking up new skills and delivering value along the way.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

  • Understanding our business and its processes through our data

Title: Graduate Data Analyst
Description:
About Hawkroot

Hawkroot connects businesses with pre-vetted data and technology professionals for project-based engagements. We help organisations across multiple sectors deliver measurable outcomes in analytics, reporting, automation, and digital transformation. We work with clients and professionals across Europe, including the Netherlands, supporting data and analytics project delivery remotely. Our team gives members direct exposure to real client projects from day one.




Role Overview

Hawkroot is seeking a Graduate Data Analyst to join our remote team on a temporary basis. This is a temporary, part-time opportunity for recent graduates based in the Netherlands who are looking to apply their analytical skills to real-world data projects and build practical experience in a professional environment.




Contract: Temporary (3 months)

Working hours: 20 hours per week, Monday to Friday

Hourly rate: €18.00 gross per hour

Work arrangement: Fully remote

Location: Netherlands




What You Will Be Doing

- Organise, clean, and prepare datasets to ensure accuracy and readiness for analysis

- Write and execute SQL queries to extract and manipulate data from relevant sources

- Perform data analysis using Microsoft Excel and summarise findings in a clear format

- Support the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports using Power BI or similar tools

- Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies within datasets and communicate findings to the team

- Assist in preparing data outputs for client-facing reports and internal documentation

- Maintain clear records of analytical processes and contribute to data quality improvements




What We Are Looking For

- A recent graduate with a degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field

- No prior professional experience required — strong coursework, personal projects, or certifications are welcomed

- Working knowledge of SQL and proficiency in Microsoft Excel

- Exposure to Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualisation platforms is beneficial

- Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and structured thinking

- Confident written and verbal communication skills in English

- Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a remote environment

- Based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands




What This Role Offers

- Direct involvement in live data projects across multiple client engagements

- Exposure to end-to-end data workflows including extraction, analysis, and reporting

- Practical experience with SQL, Excel, and Power BI in a professional environment

- Remote-first working culture with a supportive and experienced team

- A meaningful addition to your professional portfolio and CV

- Gross hourly rate of €18.00 per hour




Equal Opportunity Statement

Hawkroot is committed to equal treatment and non-discrimination across all stages of hiring. We welcome applications from recent graduates of all backgrounds, nationalities, and experiences. All candidates based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands are encouraged to apply.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

32–40 hours per week | On-site, Malta 

 

Bits Malta Ltd is looking for a commercially-minded, curious Data Analyst; someone who doesn’t just produce charts, but turns data into decisions. An honest admission: our previous Data Analyst delivered many beautiful dashboards. But dashboards don't change a business. Insights do.

 

We're looking for someone who reads every number with the question: "So what?"

 

What you'll do

Own our data warehouse. It's the place where signals surface, and action begins.
Deliver a weekly strategic scorecard to the executive team, with your interpretation and recommendations.
Translate patterns in player behaviour, marketing, compliance and cost into concrete improvements.
Proactively flag what the organisation is missing.
Support marketing, product, customer service and compliance with analyses that actually lead to action.

 

You're strong in

SQL (required), Python or R, Power BI or Tableau, and expert-level Excel.
Commercial thinking. You see data as input for decisions, not as an end in itself.
Working independently, juggling multiple questions in parallel, and owning projects from question to decision.
Excellent English, both verbal and written.
Online gambling / iGaming experience and familiarity with AML / Responsible Gambling is a strong plus.

 

What we offer

Direct exposure to executive decision-making. Your work is seen and used.
International environment in a fast-growing, regulated sector.
Room for autonomy and development.
Competitive salary, strong benefits, private health insurance and performance bonus.
Relocation support for candidates moving to Malta.

 

Screening filter

In your motivation, include one concrete example of an analysis that actually changed a business decision. Without this example, we won't consider your application.

 

Selection process

Introductory interview covering background and motivation.
Data case. You receive an anonymised dataset, formulate three commercial insights with recommendations, and present them in 15 minutes to the management team.
Final interview with the executive team.

 

Practical

On-site position at our Malta office.
Bits Malta Ltd provides operational, compliance and customer service facilitation within the online gambling sector.

 

No agency recruitment, please.

#DataAnalyst #iGaming #Malta #Hiring #BusinessIntelligence #PowerBI #SQL #ResponsibleGambling

Title: Data Analyst (Freelance | Remoto)
Description:
🚀 Estamos a recrutar | Data Analyst (Freelance | Remoto)

📍 Localização: Remoto – (candidatos devem residir em Portugal ou Espanha)




Data Analyst para projeto remoto focado em data quality, limpeza manual de dados e reconciliação entre sistemas.




🧠 O que procuramos:

🧹 Manual Data Cleansing

Identificação de duplicados e aplicação de “survivorship logic”
Padronização de nomes, endereços e campos
Preenchimento de campos obrigatórios segundo regras definidas




🗂️ Shadow IT

Trabalho com Excel e bases de dados locais
Estruturação de dados não organizados para migração




🔗 Cross-System Reconciliation

Comparação de dados entre sistemas (ex: CRM vs SAP)
Identificação de discrepâncias (“deltas”)




📋 SOPs

Execução rigorosa de Standard Operating Procedures




🌍 Inglês

Inglês avançado




✨ Diferenciais (BIG PLUS):

💼 Salesforce / Veeva / SAP

💻 SQL básico + scripting (qualquer linguagem)

🧠 Data Quality Tools (fuzzy matching / AI inference)

📊 IQVIA / OneKey




📅 Requisitos:

+3 anos de experiência em data / data quality / analytics




📩 Interessado/a? Envia a tua candidatura via LinkedIn.




🔎 Não é para ti? Partilha com alguém da tua rede 🙌




#DataAnalyst #DataQuality #RemoteJobs #Portugal #SQL #DataCleansing #Hiring #Freelance #DataGovernance

Title: Risk Data Analyst
Description:
ROLE PURPOSE

To collect, organize and interpret statistical information to transform to specific actions. You will also monitor performance and quality control plans to identify improvements.




PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

SQL querying and result visualization.
Preparing proposals to boost risk KPIs performance.
Data gathering and analysis, searching patterns.
Statistical analysis of portfolio performance
Risk KPIs monitoring and improvement actions preparation.
Monitoring and analysis of credit portfolio and credit process efficiency
Report’s creation and improvement
Analysis of external providers data to be included into statistical models.
Mathematical forecasting model preparation




KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS

Skills:

Strong analytical and mathematical skills and mind – at least 3 years of experience within Risk department analyst role, fintech/financial industry.
Strong Knowledge of and experience with databases and statistics (SQL, Python) – at least 3 years of experience.
Data visualization packages (Tableau, Looker, etc.).
Proactive approach – you have proven experience in implementing your own proposals to impact visibly Risk KPIs.
High attention to detail.
Good organisational/time management skills.
Team player.




Knowledge/Abilities:

Numerical mindset and able to get insights out of data.
Knowledge of statistics and experience using statistical packages for analyzing datasets (Python, SPSS, SAS etc.).
Methodological way of working and ability to cope with competing demands and prioritize tasks accordingly.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE

Degree in Statistics or Mathematics or a related subject involving math’s, such as economics or data science.
Fluent English - spoken and written.
At least 3 year of experience as Data Analyst / Credit Analyst within Risk Department.




BENEFITS

💪 Private health and dental insurance

👟 Discounted gym membership

💻 Freedom to work from home two days a week

🍎 Kitchen with free coffee and fruit each Monday, Wednesday and Friday and snacks

🌍 Team building activities and work in an international and passionate environment




Don’t miss the opportunity to be part of a company that values innovation and talent!




Join us and take your career to the next level 🚀

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
For a client in Amsterdam, we are looking for a Data Analyst (Marketing Analytics) on a payroll basis. In this role, you’ll sit at the heart of marketing performance, helping connect data to real business outcomes. You’ll work with both global and local stakeholders, translating complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that shape future marketing investments.


Responsibilities

  • Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards to track marketing performance, channel contribution, and ROI;
  • Work with large-scale datasets using BigQuery, dbt, and SQL to create reliable analytical pipelines;
  • Conduct both recurring and ad hoc analyses to support business decision-making;
  • Apply statistical techniques (e.g. regression, hypothesis testing) to uncover deeper insights;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams across data, marketing, and product;
  • ranslate complex findings into clear visualisations and stakeholder-ready insights;
  • Explore and apply AI tooling to improve efficiency in analytics workflows.


Requirements

  • Strong hands-on experience with Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard design);
  • Solid experience with SQL, dbt, and BigQuery;
  • Experience working with marketing data platforms;
  • Knowledge of Python for analytics and modelling is beneficial;
  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.


What We Offer

  • Interim position (payroll)
  • Hybrid working: 2–3 days per week in the Amsterdam office (non-negotiable)

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Adecco seleziona Junior Data Analyst per importante azienda operante nel settore aereoportuale.




La risorsa sarà inserita all’interno del team di riferimento e si occuperà di supportare le attività di analisi dati, pianificazione e monitoraggio delle performance aziendali, contribuendo all’ottimizzazione di risorse e processi.

Diploma o laurea in ambito economico, informatico o affini
Conoscenza approfondita di:

Power BI

Excel VBA (Visual Basic for Applications)

SharePoint

Power Apps

Power Automate

  • Elaborare dimensionamenti di risorse umane e materiali, definendo i target di produttività
  • Monitorare i livelli di produttività e analizzare eventuali scostamenti
  • Supportare le valutazioni strategiche attraverso l’utilizzo degli strumenti informatici a disposizione
  • Realizzare e implementare sistemi avanzati di pianificazione e reportistica giornaliera
  • Analizzare i dati al fine di ottimizzare risorse e processi


Offerta:

  • Inquadramento e RAL commisurati all’esperienza del candidato selezionato


La risorsa selezionata lavorerà a stretto contatto con il team di riferimento, contribuendo allo sviluppo di analisi strategiche e al miglioramento continuo delle performance aziendali.


Se sei interessato/a a crescere in un contesto dinamico e orientato ai dati, inviaci la tua candidatura.

Title: Data Analyst (Product)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists ‌work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a talented Data Analyst to dig into our complex databases, look for root causes of a problem, design your own solutions, and write your own code to implement them. We aim for you to never stop learning, picking up new skills and delivering value along the way.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through our data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and service teams
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Maintaining and creating new dashboards to track metrics and visualise insights
Promoting data literacy across the company, organising and holding workshops


What You'll Need


Previous experience in an analytical role, creating impactful solutions
A background/education in a quantitative discipline
5+ years of experience with SQL
Great skills with Python or other programming languages
Evidence of solid mathematical and statistics knowledge


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Data & BI Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 28,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.5 billion euros in 2023.

Job Description


Creation of dashboards and reports to support decision-making.
Implementation of data visualization strategies.
Data Governance: asset cataloging, application of technical and functional rules.
Data Quality: execution of profiles and alerts for stakeholders.
Connections with Microsoft Fabric, Dynamics, and other sources.
Support to business teams in Purview, Data Governance, and Data Quality.
Onboarding and training


Qualifications


Between 2 and 5 years of experience in the role.
Experience in Data Governance and EDP projects.
Tools and platforms:
Microsoft Purview (implementation of data quality rules)
Microsoft Fabric
Power BI
Azure
Databricks
AWS
Languages and tools:
SQL
Advanced Excel
Dashboard tools
Desirable knowledge:
SAS Enterprise Guide
DOMO
DAMAn framework
Experience in CI/CD and agile methodologies.


Additional Information

Work model: Hybrid.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
🔋 AWS Data Engineer 🔋

📍 Amsterdam | 🏡 Hybrid (1-2 days in the office – your choice!)

💰 Up to €100k per annum + Amazing Benefits!

💡 Use Data to make a positive impact!




Join a Team That Feels Like Home — and Builds Like Pros!




Looking for a place where your talent is valued, your voice matters, and your impact is real? This boutique data & AI consultancy, founded in 2019, is redefining what it means to build a modern tech consultancy. Independent, profitable, and growing fast — this is a company where your career doesn’t get lost in layers of management or corporate politics.




You’ll be part of a close-knit team of experts who bring deep technical knowledge and heart to everything they do. The company partners with large, data-rich organizations across Europe — helping them navigate complexity and make faster, smarter decisions through practical data engineering and AI solutions. Think high-impact work, zero fluff.




💡 What You'll Be Doing as a AWS Data Engineer:

Develop scalable data pipelines and APIs powering GenAI-driven applications.
Develop infra-as-code using AWS CDK and CI/CD tooling.
Collaborate closely with AI engineers, architects, and product teams
Help clients to build and productionize product., dealing with a wide spectrum of Data, Cloud Infrastructure and DevOps-related problems.




🛠️ What You Bring as the Successful AWS Data Engineer:

5+ years of commercial experience in Python.
2+ years of production experience with PySpark and Kafka.
Strong knowledge of AWS services & infrastructure patterns.
Experience with asynchronous APIs using FastAPI.
Motivated by client problems, and find joy in creating simple, elegant solutions that avoid unnecessary complexity.




🎁 Benefits for the AWS Data Engineer:

Annual bonus tied to personal and company performance.
Reimbursements for internet, commuting, and work tools — no red tape.
Top-tier MacBook Pro to do your best work.
An all-expenses-paid annual retreat with the whole team (last year? A Yacht in Turkey!).
Donation matching — we’ll match up to €500/year to causes you care about.
Learning budget with access to MasterClass and more — whether it’s cooking like a pro or mastering DJ skills.
Fully paid parental leave — for all new parents.
Holiday gifts and surprises (we love giving back).
Free 1Password family plan to keep your digital life secure.




🎯 Make an Impact. Stay Curious. Work with Brilliant People.

If you’re ready to level up your career and use data to make a meaningful difference, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out for a chat! 👋

Title: Impiegato/a Data Entry – Inserimento Ordini e Gestione Anagrafiche
HTML:
Azienda operante nel settore retail e distribuzione al dettaglio ricerca una risorsa da inserire nel ruolo di Data Entry, dedicata all’inserimento e all’aggiornamento dei dati relativi a clienti, fornitori e ordini di vendita.

Attività principali:

Registrazione degli ordini all’interno del gestionale aziendale;

Aggiornamento delle anagrafiche clienti e verifica della correttezza dei dati;

Supporto all’ufficio vendite nella gestione documentale e nel monitoraggio delle richieste;

Controllo dell’allineamento tra ordini, giacenze e disponibilità di magazzino;

Archiviazione digitale e organizzazione dei file interni secondo le procedure aziendali.

Requisiti richiesti:

Diploma di scuola secondaria di secondo grado;

Buona conoscenza degli strumenti informatici (Excel, e-mail, gestionali aziendali);

Precisione e metodo nella gestione dei dati;

Capacità di lavorare in team e di rispettare scadenze operative;

Attenzione ai dettagli e senso di responsabilità.

Offerta e condizioni:

Contratto a tempo determinato, con orario full time dal lunedì al venerdì;

Formazione iniziale retribuita e supporto operativo continuo;

Collaborazione quotidiana con colleghi per la gestione delle attività assegnate.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
🔔 Data Engineer

💰 €75k - €100k + 5–25% annual bonus

📍 Amsterdam / Utrecht / Rotterdam




Most Data Engineering roles keep the lights on. This one helps decide billion-euro deals.




If you're a Data Engineer who wants real ownership, high-stakes technical challenges, and direct business impact, this is the kind of role that rarely comes to market. A leading boutique in data-driven financial due diligence is hiring a Data Engineer to help transform how M&A decisions are made.




Every year, their teams support 150+ acquisition investigations for private equity firms, investment funds, and corporates, using data, AI, and cloud technology to uncover what others miss.

This isn’t another “build pipelines and maintain dashboards” job.




This is your chance to sit at the core of a highly specialised Data Science function, building the infrastructure that powers live deal intelligence in one of the most intense environments in finance.




What makes this Data Engineer role different?

You’ll be:

✅ Designing cloud-native data architectures

✅ Building scalable pipelines from the ground up

✅ Developing tools that give analysts a decisive edge during live M&A negotiations

✅ Making real architectural decisions from day one

✅ Embedding AI into financial analysis workflows

You’ll also collaborate on a proprietary BI platform already used by leading investment firms across the Netherlands.




What the successful Data Engineer needs:

✔️ 3+ years in Data Engineering or Data Architecture.

✔️ Strong Python, SQL, and Azure expertise.

✔️ An entrepreneurial mindset and confidence working autonomously.

✔️ Fluency in Dutch and English.




🎁 What’s on offer:

💰 €75k - €100k + 5–25% annual bonus

📍 Amsterdam / Utrecht / Rotterdam

🏡 Hybrid working after onboarding (2–3 office days)

🏖️ 30 days holiday

🤝 High-performance, close-knit culture with genuine team energy

👤 Who they’re looking for




Plus something engineers genuinely value: the freedom to make the right technical decisions yourself.




Why this stands out for a Data Engineer:

🔍 Real complexity — every dataset, deal, and deadline is different

🤖 AI at the core — automation and intelligence built into workflows

🏛️ Unique domain — M&A due diligence is fast-moving, technical, and commercially critical

🛠️ Technical autonomy — shape the engineering direction, not just execute tickets

📈 Clear progression — senior engineering or technical leadership in a scaling business




💬 If you’re ready to build systems that influence major investment decisions, let’s talk.

Title: Administrativo CSC
Description:
En Urbaser potenciamos el valor de los recursos del planeta cada día, para construir un mañana más sostenible. Somos líderes globales en ofrecer un modelo integral de soluciones medioambientales para los ciudadanos, las empresas y la sociedad. Además, nuestra naturaleza de compañía global, con presencia en alrededor de 15 países, nos permite impulsar una circularidad real. Nuestro objetivo es crear un futuro mejor, desafiando juntos los estándares actuales y anticipando soluciones, haciendo que todo funcione a lo largo de la cadena de valor para impulsar una transformación relevante.

Actualmente buscamos incorporar a nuestro equipo de Servicios Compartidos de Madrid un/a Administrativo. La persona seleccionada estará en Vallecas.

¿Qué ofrecemos?


Contrato indefinido y plan de carrera.
Retribución económica y plan de retribución flexible competitivo.
Modalidad de trabajo híbrida y horario flexible.
La oportunidad de trabajar y aprender junto a profesionales líderes en sus áreas de conocimiento
La oportunidad de ganar experiencia en un sector en auge: la sostenibilidad y la gestión de residuos.


¿Quiénes somos?

En Urbaser somos uno de los líderes mundiales en soluciones medioambientales, una compañía global orientada a potenciar el valor de los recursos del planeta para construir un mañana más sostenible. A través de nuestros servicios de limpieza y recogida, atendemos a más de 64 millones de personas alrededor de 15 países a través de una enorme red de más de 50.000 empleados y 150 plantas que impulsan cada día una circularidad real.

En Urbaser estamos comprometidos con el principio de absoluta igualdad de oportunidades, promoviendo la presencia equilibrada entre mujeres y hombres y fomentando la inclusión laboral de colectivos en situación de vulnerabilidad; siendo especialmente bienvenidas las candidaturas para ocupar puestos en actividades con infrarrepresentación femenina. Respetamos la diversidad de género, discapacidad, edad, origen étnico, cultural o racial, estado civil, así como la identidad y orientación sexual de las personas que presenten sus candidaturas.

Si estás buscando desarrollarte profesionalmente en una empresa en pleno crecimiento, ¡Te estamos esperando!

Title: Atención al cliente y área online
Description:
Ofrecemos la posibilidad de construir historia en una empresa icónica aportando valor en el área online y de atención al cliente de SARGADELOS.

Durante generaciones hemos creado piezas que trascienden al producto y buscamos personas que quieran seguir construyendo historia ayudando a llevar el arte, diseño y cultura gallegas al mundo a través del canal digital.

Atención al cliente y área online

Buscamos una persona con experiencia en ecommerce y atención al cliente, capaz de ofrecer una experiencia online excelente, cuidada y coherente con la identidad histórica y estética de Sargadelos. Una persona que entienda que en Sargadelos no solo se trabaja cada día para vender productos, sino también para seguir construyendo legado.

INSCRÍBETE


Ubicación


Cervo (Lugo), Galicia


Tipo de contrato


Indefinido


Salario


A convenir

Sobre nosotros

En Sargadelos somos una empresa con más de dos siglos de historia dedicada a la fabricación de porcelana de alta calidad, combinando tradición artesanal y diseño vanguardista. Nuestro compromiso con el detalle, la innovación y la cultura gallega nos ha convertido en un referente internacional en el sector.

Trabajamos en un entorno donde cada pieza es única, cuidando cada fase del proceso productivo y fomentando la creatividad y la expresión artística.

Qué te ofrecemos


Incorporación a una empresa consolidada con reconocimiento internacional
Entorno creativo y artístico con impacto cultural
Formación continua en técnicas cerámicas y procesos artesanales
Estabilidad laboral y desarrollo profesional
Participación en proyectos de diseño innovadores
Beneficios sociales y descuentos en productos de la marca


Qué necesitamos

QUÉ BUSCAMOS

Experiencia en ecommerce, gestión online y atención al cliente.

Conocimiento de plataformas como Shopify.

Capacidad de organización, autonomía y orientación al detalle.

Buen nivel de comunicación escrita y trato excelente con el cliente.

Sensibilidad estética y cuidado por la imagen de marca.

Experiencia gestionando pedidos, incidencias y soporte postventa.

Se valorarán conocimientos de:


email marketing
redes sociales

Title: Data Center Technician
Description:
Overview

As a Microsoft Data Center Technician (DCT), you will stage, set up and perform hardware deployments. In addition, you will also perform troubleshooting and diagnostics on equipment, as well as perform hardware decommissions for simple changes and refreshes following Standard Operating Procedures. This opportunity will allow you to deepen your understanding of Data Center standard processes and procedures, develop mentoring skills while providing guidance to peers, familiarizing yourself with security and data management procedures, and accelerate your career growth in the process.

Microsoft’s Cloud Operations & Innovation (CO+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CO+I DCT, you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO+I is focused on the personal and professional development of all employees and offers training and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity & Inclusion training and events, and professional certifications.

Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 200 Data Centers in 32 countries and millions of servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide.

With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our data center design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world-class cloud provider.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities


Performs diagnostics and troubleshooting following standard procedures, quickly identifies the cause(s) of issues, and replaces faulty components with minimal customer and business disruption.
Performs post-execution quality checks and verifies that grounding, staging, labeling, and cabling are set up properly according to safety protocols, deployment standards, and planned Network Design Tasks (NDTs).
Decommissions hardware for simple changes and refreshes (e.g., memory upgrades, rebuilds) following standard procedures with minimal guidance.
Follows procedures to communicate, report, and escalate incidents to appropriate Microsoft data center operations management units, Technician Leads, and engineering specialists.
Assists and provides guidance to other technicians to complete challenging or complex tasks.
Completes required training aligned to the role and workload; observes more experienced technicians to gain hands-on experience and relevant on-the-job training.
Contributes to a positive and effective team environment by sharing information with others, contributing to regular team meetings, asking questions, and staying apprised of the status of others' work.
Has pride and a sense of accountability for the service quality, completeness, and resulting user experience; displays accountability and ownership of the data center facilities.
Embodies our culture and values.


Qualifications

Required Qualifications:


High school diploma, GED, or equivalent and basic knowledge of computer hardware and components AND some experience supporting IT equipment or related technology.
A valid drivers licence for the country posting is required due to the remote location and future on call duties.


Background Check Requirements

Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings:


Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter.


While not required, we also look for the following Preferred Qualifications:


Solid experience supporting IT equipment or related technology.
Applicable certifications: CompTIA (A+, Server+, Network+), Basic Structure Cabling (BSC).


This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

Title: Data Analyst F/H
Description:
Au sein de la Direction de la Transformation Digitale, vos principales missions seront les suivantes




Analyse et Modélisation des Données :

Collecter, traiter et analyser des ensembles de données complexes provenant de diverses sources (bases de données, API, fichiers logs, etc.).
Développer et optimiser des modèles statistiques et des algorithmes d’apprentissage automatique (machine learning) pour prédire des tendances et optimiser les processus opérationnels (contrôle de cohérence, détection de la fraude par exemple).
Évaluer et améliorer la performance des modèles existants pour assurer leur efficacité et leur pertinence.




Exploration des Données et Visualisation :

Explorer les données pour découvrir des tendances, des anomalies et des corrélations significatives.
Créer des visualisations de données interactives et informatives pour aider à la prise de décision.




Développement de Solutions Innovantes :

Collaborer avec les équipes de développement et de recherche pour concevoir et tester des prototypes de solutions basées sur les données (POC), une expérience en IA serait considérée comme un atout.
Participer à la conception et au développement de nouveaux produits et services basés sur les données.




Recherche et Développement :

Effectuer une veille technologique dans les domaines de la science des données, des statistiques et du machine learning.
Proposer des solutions innovantes pour résoudre les défis opérationnels et techniques rencontrés par l’entreprise




Collaboration interservices :

Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les équipes métier de l’entreprise (logistique, achats, opérations, R&D, CRM, finance) pour identifier et répondre à leurs besoins en matière de données.
Faciliter la communication entre les équipes techniques et non techniques, en traduisant les résultats analytiques en recommandations exploitables.




Profil recherché :

Formation : Bac +5 en Data Science/Data Analyst, statistiques, informatique décisionnelle, ou équivalent.

Expérience : A partir de 3 ans d’expérience en tant que Data Analyst, idéalement en PME/ETI ou en cabinet de conseil.

Maîtrise des outils de data visualisation tels que SAC, Power BI, Quicksight ou équivalent.
Expertise en langages de programmation tels que Python, R, JAVA ou SQL.
Connaissance des bases de données relationnelles et/ou cloud (AWS).
Expérience en machine learning.
Connaissance des concepts et des outils d’IA
Intérêt marqué pour la transformation digitale et l’accompagnement du changement.
Esprit analytique et rigoureux, goût pour la résolution de problèmes.
Aptitude à vulgariser et communiquer efficacement avec des profils non techniques.
Proactivité, autonomie et curiosité intellectuelle.
Bon relationnel et goût pour le travail en équipe transverse.




Chez ecosystem, nous croyons en la diversité et l’inclusion, et nous nous engageons à offrir des opportunités équitables à tous. Cette offre d’emploi est ouverte à toutes les personnes qualifiées, y compris celles en situation de handicap.
